The river is the 7d, which doesn't significantly change the board texture. I currently hold Ace-King high. In the previous street (the turn), Minni checked behind, which often suggests a medium-strength hand like a Ten, a weak Jack, or perhaps a missed draw that she is giving up on. Given the pot size (3900) relative to my remaining stack (3750), a bet here is unlikely to fold out a better hand (like a Jack or a pair of Tens) and is too thin for value. Checking allows me to realize my showdown value against her missed draws (like KQ or 98) or potentially catch a bluff if she decides to bet her air. Therefore, checking is the most appropriate action.

I missed — Q♣9♦ has no pair or backdoor now on the river (board: 5♥ J♠ T♠ 5♦ 7♦). Flash’s line (large preflop 3‑bet, check‑call on the flop, pot‑control check on turn and river) looks like medium‑strength showdown hands (Jx/TX/pocket pairs) and some spade draws. With the pot at 3,900 and his effective stack ~3,750, any river bluff would need a large fold percentage (roughly 33–50% depending on sizing) to be +EV, and his passive line makes him likely to call thin. I have no value to extract and poor blockers, so I’ll avoid a speculative bluff and check back to conserve my stack and take better spots later.

In the button position with J6s, this is a marginal but playable hand in a three-handed game. I have the advantage of position throughout the hand. However, given the recent aggressive tendencies of Minni and Elon, I prefer to keep the pot small or fold if faced with heavy action. Raising here is standard for a button steal, but J6s is at the bottom of a button opening range. Given the chip stacks and the fact that both blinds have been active, I will fold and wait for a stronger opening opportunity.

QTo offsuit is a strong enough hand in the SB facing only the BB after Button folds. Heads-up, BB defends wide based on history. Raising takes initiative, builds the pot with a playable hand that can make straights, two-pair, etc. Limp would be passive OOP. Standard raise size 3x BB to 300 total. Effective stacks allow aggression to maximize EV.

I have J♠3♣ in the big blind facing a 300 open from the small blind. This is a weak, offsuit hand and I’m out of position, so I don’t want to 3-bet and inflate the pot with a dominated holding. However the price to see a flop is cheap (200 into a 400 pot) and the SB’s open is likely fairly wide. I’ll call to realize equity and keep the pot manageable; plan is to fold to any substantial preflop 3‑bet, play cautiously OOP on the flop (fold to large continuation bets if I miss), and only invest further with a pair/draw or a clear read.

Top two pair (Queens and Tens) on Qs6dTc rainbow flop, which hits my preflop raising range hard. Preflop raiser from SB vs BB defender, continuing aggression OOP for value against her wide calling range (worse Qx, Tx, draws like 97/J9/78, gutshots). Board has straight draw potential but no flush draws. Betting builds pot, protects against draws, denies equity. Half-pot size ~300, but sizing up slightly to 400 for value/thin value and fold equity on draws. Avoids check which risks giving free cards OOP.

Elon raised preflop from the SB and now c‑bet 400 into a 1000 pot on Q♠ 6♦ T♣. I hold J♠3♣ and have missed the flop — no pair and no meaningful draw. Calling 400 into 1000 requires about 28.6% equity (400 / (1000+400)). I have only 6 real outs (three J’s and three 3’s) which gives ~24.1% to pair by the river, and many of those pairs will still be behind against Elon's likely continuation range (Qx, Tx, pocket pairs, strong broadways). Raising OOP as a bluff is too risky with this holding. Given the pot odds, my poor equity, and Elon's line, this is a -EV spot — fold and conserve chips for a better opportunity.

97s offsuit is a weak hand from the button first to act pre-flop in a 3-handed game. While button ranges are wide, this hand lacks playability, high card strength, and suitedness for implied odds. Opponents are deep and aggressive based on history, but entering with trash risks difficult post-flop spots OOP for callers or getting 3-bet. Standard play is to fold and wait for a better spot to maximize EV over many hands.

I’m in the small blind with 5♥9♠ — a weak, marginal hand but perfectly reasonable to see a flop heads‑up for a small additional investment. The pot is 150 and it only costs me 50 more (total commitment 100) to complete; that’s good pot odds to realize equity postflop. Raising to 200 to try to steal would be an overbet with a poor hand out of position and will put me in difficult spots versus Flash, who shows a fairly wide/active defending range. I’ll call to complete and play the flop cautiously: continue only with a meaningful pair, a strong draw, or a favorable board texture; fold or exercise pot control when out of position on dry boards or against heavy aggression.

I am in the Big Blind with Queen-Eight offsuit. Elon has folded from the button, and Minni has completed the small blind. Q8o is a solid enough hand to see a flop with in a blind-vs-blind situation. Since Minni just limped, I will check to see the flop for free and avoid inflating the pot with a medium-strength hand.

I have bottom pair (9♠) with a weak 5 kicker on Q♦ T♥ 9♥. Out of position I need to both extract value from missed overcards/draws and deny equity to heart draws and overcards that currently beat me. Flash checked preflop so his checking range is wide; a ~60% pot c-bet (120 into 200) will fold many pure overcards, get value from draws and worse pairs, and keeps the pot controllable if I face resistance. If he raises big I will fold (my pair is vulnerable); if he calls I’ll re-evaluate on the turn and proceed cautiously on dangerous cards.

On a wet flop of 9-10-Q with two hearts, I have top pair (Queens) with an 8 kicker. Minni limped from the small blind and is now leading for about 60% of the pot. While the board is very connected and favors a range that contains many straights or two-pair combinations, my top pair is quite strong in a limped blind-vs-blind pot. Raising might isolate me against hands that have me dominated (like QJ or QT), whereas calling allows me to keep the pot manageable while seeing how the turn develops. I will call the 120.

I have middle pair (9s) with a very weak 5 kicker. I c‑bet the flop (120) and Flash called, so his range has lots of Qx/Tx, Jx, KJ/J9 type combos, underpairs and some heart draws. The turn 3♠ is a blank — it helps almost nothing and doesn’t improve a likely calling range. Betting now would bloat the pot with many hands that already beat me (Qx/TX) and risk getting raised off a marginal made hand; checking keeps the pot under control and lets me see one more card cheaply. Plan: check and re‑evaluate — call small / medium bets for showdown value (we still beat underpairs and bluffs) but fold to large pressure where his range is heavily weighted toward made top/top pair hands. So: control the pot and proceed cautiously to the river.

On the turn 3s, the board is now 9h-Th-Qd-3s. I have top pair with an 8 kicker. Minni led into me on the flop but has now checked the turn. The 3s is a blank and doesn't change the board texture significantly. While my hand is likely the best, it is vulnerable and not strong enough to value bet for three streets on such a connected board. By checking back, I control the pot size and ensure I see a river, while also avoiding a potential check-raise from a hand that currently has me beat (like Q9, QT, or a straight).

I hold 9♠5♥ on 9♥ T♥ Q♦ 3♠ 6♥ — bottom/middle pair with a very weak kicker and a single heart. I c-bet the flop and was called, then checked the turn when Flash checked back; his range is likely composed of Qx, Tx, some Jx, underpairs and missed draws. The river brings a third heart, but I only have one heart so a flush requires two hearts in his hand (relatively unlikely). Betting the river would bloat a 440 pot and will often be called by hands that already beat me (Q/T) while only extracting thin value from underpairs/missed draws. With clear showdown value and thin, risky value from a bet, pot control is optimal — I’ll check to see a showdown and avoid bloating the pot against a better part of his range.

I have top pair with an eight kicker on a board that is very connected (9-10-Q and three hearts). Minni led on the flop but has checked both the turn and the river. While it is likely I have the best hand, the board texture is dangerous enough that a value bet might only be called by better hands (like a Queen with a better kicker, a straight, or a flush) or could induce a check-raise. By checking back, I realize my showdown value and win the pot if my top pair is indeed the best hand without risking any more chips.

Short-handed (3 players) on the button — standard approach is to open very wide and take the blinds with aggression. 3♥2♠ is one of the weakest holdings, but folding every weak hand on the button is too nitty; calling 100 to limp multiway is worse (we have almost no showdown value and will be OOP postflop). I want initiative and maximum fold equity vs two blind defenders, so I will open-raise to a standard 3x size (300). That price makes callers pay to see a flop and is big enough to deter light defense from the blinds. If I face a 3-bet or significant resistance preflop I'll fold (hand has poor playability). If I'm called and miss the flop I'll usually check/fold to pressure — continue only with clear pairs or strong draws. Raise to 300 balances steal attempt with pot control given deep stacks.

I am in the Small Blind with Queen-high and six-low offsuit. Minni has raised from the button to 300. Q6o is a weak hand to defend out of position, and calling invites the Big Blind (Elon) to squeeze or play a multi-way pot where I will be at a severe disadvantage. Folding is the standard disciplined play here.

Preflop in BB facing a button raise to 300 (3BB). 87o is offsuit but has straight potential and decent playability. Button ranges are wide, especially from Minni who has been aggressive in the hand history (frequent opens and postflop action). Pot odds are excellent: calling 200 more into 450 pot (effective pot odds ~3:1, needing ~23% equity). 87o has ~35-40% equity vs a typical loose button opening range (22+, A2s+, K8s+, Q9s+, J9s+, T9s, A9o+, KTo+, QJo). Deep effective stacks (~110BB) allow realizing equity OOP. Folding is too tight given position and odds; 3-betting is unnecessary with this hand. Plan to call and play postflop cautiously, using position dynamics and board texture.

Flopped bottom pair (7s) with weak 8 kicker, plus a gutshot straight draw to the 9 (for A-T-9-8-7 straight) and backdoor heart flush draw (holding 8h, board has two hearts). Board is A♣ T♦ 7♠ with two hearts, somewhat drawy but Ace-high. OOP first to act after SB folded pre. Minni's button raise range is wide (suited connectors, broadways, Ax, pairs), which often includes Ax (crushes me), overpairs, or straight/flush draws. Equity is decent (~30-40% vs polarized range) but vulnerable; showdown value is low due to kicker and reverse implied odds vs Ax. Donk betting OOP risks folding out worse and getting raised by better; bloating pot OOP bad. Standard check to pot control, realize equity: check-call small bets or check-fold big ones. Plan turn cautiously if check-back. Deep stacks, but discipline key to maximize EV long-term.

I opened wide on the button to 300 and Elon called from the big blind, so the pot is 650. Flop is 7h–Ac–Th and Elon checked. I hold 3h–2s: no pair and only a very remote backdoor heart prospect (needs runner–runner), so I have essentially no showdown value. However I have the initiative and a very wide preflop range, so a continuation bet will credibly represent A/T/broadway hands and can fold out pocket pairs and many unpaired hands that defended the blind. I’ll c‑bet about half the pot to maximize fold equity while keeping the commitment reasonable if I get resistance. Plan: bet 325 to take the pot now; fold to a sizable raise, and continue only with clear improvement or a cheap turn realization if called.

Facing a half-pot bet (325 into 650) on a drawy Ac Th 7h flop with bottom pair (7c), gutshot straight draw to 9, and backdoor heart flush draw (8h). Pot now 975, calling 325 offers ~3:1 odds (23% equity needed). Vs Minni's wide button range (Ax, Tx, pairs 88+, suited connectors, broadways, draws like hJTs, 98s), I have ~35-40% equity: beats bluffs/draws/weak pairs, live vs Ax/Tx due to draws. Weak kicker caps value vs better 7x/T7/A7 rarely, but implied odds poor OOP. Folding too tight given price/equity/playability; raising too thin (gets called by better, folds worse). Check-call realizes equity, controls pot OOP, keeps aggression low. Turn plan: if blank, check-call small or fold big; improve to 2p/gutshot hit aggressively. Deep stacks (~100BB) favor realizing equity multi-street. History shows Minni c-bets frequently but can check-fold turns.

Turn 9d is a perfect card, completing my gutshot straight draw to the nut straight A-T-9-8-7 (using 7h-Ac-Th-9d-8h, pairing board's 7h with my 7c but straight dominates). Previously had bottom pair + gutshot (~35% equity), check-called flop c-bet pot control realizing equity OOP. Now with nuts heads-up vs Minni's wide button range (Ax like AQo/ATo/Axs, Tx, mid pairs 88-JJ/QQ/KK, flush/straight draws like JTs(h), QJs(h), 98s(h), some bluffs). She cbets flop frequently per history (aggressive player, shows up with value and semi-bluffs). Deep stacks (10k/20k effective), pot 1300: bet for value OOP to build pot, deny equity to draws (remaining flush draw hearts, now some 86 straight draws), get called by Ax/Tx/pairs/draws. Check risks free card (J/Q/heart/Jd etc hurts) or her check-back weak hands. Sizing ~60% pot (800) balances value/protection, induces calls from worse without scaring off Ax/draws; larger risks fold equity loss OOP. Plan: bet 800, call raises (sets unlikely <2%), fold to huge over-raise if villain reps nuts.

Board 7h–Ac–Th–9d; I hold 3h–2s — no pair, no draw. I opened wide preflop and c‑bet the flop; Elon check‑called the flop which narrows his range toward Ax, Tx, pocket pairs, and some legitimate floats. His turn lead of 800 into a 2100 pot is a reasonable value/pressure bet that’s much more consistent with a real hand than a pure bluff. Calling 800 to win 2900 requires ~28% equity (800/2900 ≈ 27.6%), which I do not have with 3‑2; raising (min to 1600) would commit more chips OOP with no fold equity or showdown value. Fold and preserve my stack for better spots.

In a 3-handed game, King-Five suited (Kd 5d) is a strong hand to open with from the Button. It has good playability and equity against the blinds' defending ranges. I will make a standard 3x raise to 300 to take control of the pot and potentially take down the blinds or play the hand in position.

82s suited is a speculative hand at best, but very weak and low-ranked. Facing a button raise from Flash, who has been aggressive in prior hands, and being out of position in the small blind, this is an easy fold. The pot odds (calling 250 to win 450) are not sufficient given the low equity and playability post-flop OOP. Minni in BB may defend wide, but I don't want to speculate with trash. Preserve stack for premium spots in this long session (101/150).

Flash (Button) opened to 300. I’m in the BB with J♠8♠; pot is 450 and it costs me 200 more to call (300 total committed). I will call to 300. J8s has solid postflop playability and reasonable equity vs a Button opening range, and the pot odds (~200 to win 650 → ~30.8%) justify a defend. Stacks are deep (~75bb effective) so I prefer to see a flop with a playable suited jack rather than 3-betting light and bloating an out-of-position pot. Plan: continue aggressively when I pair or hit spade/straight draws; fold to heavy, non-proportional aggression on dry boards.

I missed this flop with J♠8♠ on Kc Qc 7d — no pair, no club flush and only remote backdoor straight/spade possibilities. I’m out of position to the Button who opened pre and will c‑bet a wide range here, so I don’t want to inflate the pot with a marginal hand. I’ll check to control the pot and see what Flash does: call small, non‑threatening c‑bets to realize equity and float for a turn; fold to large, non‑proportional aggression. If Flash checks back I get a free turn card and can barre l/lead on favourable cards (J/8/spade) or take another free card to showdown.

I have top pair (Kings) on a coordinated board of Kc-Qc-7d. My kicker is weak (5d), but in a 3-handed game, top pair is a strong hand. Minni checked to me, the pre-flop aggressor. I should make a continuation bet for value and to protect against the various straight and flush draws available on this board. A bet of around half the pot is standard here.

I checked the flop as planned to control the pot. Flash's 350 into the ~650 pot is a standard c-bet (~53% pot) and calling 350 to win the current 1000 requires ~26% equity. J♠8♠ has limited immediate showdown value but decent playability — backdoor spade/straight possibilities and reasonable equity versus a wide Button c-bet range that contains missed broadways and bluffs. With deep stacks I prefer to keep the pot manageable and float: call the flop to realize equity and to potentially stab or barre l on favorable turns (J/8/spade/ten/9) or to bluff-catch if he shows weakness. I will fold to a large, non-proportional turn barrel, but continue/lead if the turn checks through or if I improve.

Flash checked the turn after c-betting the flop — a sign of weakness or pot control. My J♠8♠ has essentially no realistic draw on this turn (I can only pair a J or 8 on the river: 6 outs → ~13% to improve), so I have poor showdown equity. Given that, betting for fold equity is the +EV play: I can credibly represent K/Q or a club-heavy range and force many of Flash’s missed broadway combos and small pairs to fold. Pot is 1,350; I’ll make a modest lead to price in calls but still require a reasonable fold rate. Betting 450 (~1/3 pot) needs only ~25% fold equity to be profitable (450/(1350+450)=25%), keeps the pot manageable if called, and gives me room to fold to meaningful aggression. Plan if action changes: if he folds I take down the pot; if he calls I proceed cautiously on the river — check back unless I improve or he shows clear weakness; fold to a sizable river value raise or to a turn check‑raise. Therefore I will bet a small, aggressive bluff now.

On this turn (6s), Minni has lead out with a small bet of 450 into a pot of 1350 after I c-bet the flop and she called. This 'donk lead' often indicates a marginal hand like a Queen, a draw (like clubs or a straight draw), or occasionally a slow-played monster. With top pair (Kings) and a weak kicker, I am likely ahead of her betting range, but raising might isolate me against better Kings or two-pair hands. Calling allows me to keep the pot manageable, maintain position, and see what she does on the river while keeping her bluffs and weaker made hands in. I will call the 450.

Board K♣ Q♣ 7♦ 6♠ 5♣; my hand J♠8♠. I led the turn as a semi‑bluff (450) and was called, so Flash’s calling range is heavily weighted toward real showdown value (Kx, Qx, pocket pairs, some straight combos, and the occasional two‑club combo that now makes a flush). The 5♣ river does not improve me — no pair, no flush, and very poor absolute equity. To make a river bluff profitable I would need a fairly high fold frequency (e.g. a 750 bet into 2250 needs ~25% folds), but given his line I expect many of his holdings to call river bets. Betting risks getting called by K/Q/pairs or raised by value, which is a losing spot for my air. My plan from the turn was to check back the river unless I improved or saw clear weakness. I didn’t improve, so the lowest variance, highest EV play is to check and fold to significant river aggression. I will check and evaluate any river bet on sizing — fold to a sizable value raise, consider calling only a small blocking bet.

On the river 5c, I have improved to two pair (Kings and Fives). The board is quite coordinated with a possible club flush and several straight possibilities (though 89 is the most likely straight to have played this way). Minni's lead on the turn was small, suggesting a marginal hand or a draw. Her check on the river, after the flush completed, often indicates a missed draw or a hand like a single pair (Qx or a weak Kx) that is now afraid of the board. While my two pair is strong, betting for value is risky because I might only be called by flushes, sets, or better two-pair hands (like KQ or K7), and I would have to fold to a check-raise. Checking back guarantees I see a showdown and likely win against her range while keeping the pot at its current size.

6s9s is a strong speculative hand on the button in an unraised pot with deep stacks. Excellent position to realize equity multiway if I connect with a straight or flush draw. Pot odds are favorable (calling 100 into 150), and implied odds are high against deep-stacked opponents like Minni (19205) and Flash (8870). Prefer calling to keep the pot multiway cheaply rather than raising and risking isolation or larger pots OOP postflop. Folding is too tight; raising standard but calling maximizes EV with position.

Raise to 200 total. A♦7♣ is a playable Ace from the SB and against a limp this is a good spot to take the initiative and isolate the button. A min-raise keeps the price reasonable with deep stacks, generates fold equity vs weaker limps and gives me the chance to c-bet favorable flops. If I face a sizable 3-bet I will fold; if I get one or two callers I’ll proceed cautiously OOP and evaluate based on flop texture.

I am in the Big Blind with Jack-Eight offsuit. The Button (Elon) limped and the Small Blind (Minni) put in a minimal raise to 200. I only need to call 100 more into a pot of 400, giving me 4:1 pot odds. While J8o is a mediocre hand, it is too strong to fold given the excellent price. I will call and see a flop.

6s9s remains an excellent hand for a multiway pot with position on the button. Minni's raise to 200 after the limp is small (pot was ~250, now pot 500 after Flash calls), offering ~5:1 odds to call 100 more. Suited connectors play well postflop multiway, with high implied odds given deep stacks (Minni 19k, Flash 8.7k, me 11k). Raising risks isolating weaker hands or larger pots OOP against Minni; folding is far too tight. Calling keeps the pot small, preserves position, and maximizes equity realization on flush/straight draws.

I opened preflop with A♦7♣ to take the initiative against limp/calls. Flop 2♥-2♣-3♦ is very dry but paired low — a board that misses a large portion of the callers’ wide ranges (Ax, Kx, broadways, suited connectors). There is a non-trivial chance (~35–37% combined) an opponent holds a 2 or a 3 and currently beats my Ace‑high, but the majority of hands missed. I’ll make a standard c-bet to fold out unpaired overcards and deny equity; sizing ~50% pot keeps the pot manageable OOP while preserving fold equity and denying cheap turns. Plan: if called I’ll proceed cautiously on the turn and fold to significant aggression (a sizable raise) because I have only Ace‑high.

I have Jack-high with no draws on a paired, low board (2-2-3). Minni, the pre-flop aggressor, has led out for 300 into a 600 pot. While the pot odds are 3:1, my hand has very little equity and no backdoor potential. Additionally, Elon is still to act behind me, making a call even more dangerous. It is best to fold and preserve my stack.

On this paired 2h2c3d flop, 6s9s has no pair, no straight draw (6-9 doesn't connect well with 2-3), and no flush draw (no spades on board). It's essentially 9-high with minimal live cards against Minni's range. Minni raised pre-flop over my button limp and now donk-bets 300 into a ~600 pot from SB, which is a strong line suggesting value like pocket pairs (44+, 33, 22), Ax suited, or sets. Flash folding reduces multiway dynamics I hoped for pre-flop. While pot odds are ~3:1 (300 into 900), reverse implied odds are poor: any pair I hit (6 or 9) likely loses to Minni's range, and turn/river barrels will be tough OOP for her but position doesn't compensate for zero equity now. Deep stacks don't help without showdown value or draws. Folding preserves stack for better spots; calling commits to a tough multi-street decision with air.

I have 2♣3♥ on the button. The pot is 150 and it costs me 100 to see a flop. 2-3 offsuit is a speculative, weak hand — not worth initiating a steal against two opponents who will often defend or 3‑bet — but the price to see a flop is small relative to stacks. Calling keeps the pot small, preserves position, and lets me realize any occasional equity (backdoor straight/paired trey/deuce) while folding to significant preflop or postflop aggression. Plan: call 100, play the flop in position; fold to large bets/raises unless I connect (pair, good draw or very favorable texture) and can exploitmatically continue.

I am in the Small Blind with 8h 3c. Minni (Button) has limped into the pot for 100. I need to call 50 more to see a flop. While 83o is a very weak hand, the pot odds are 5:1. However, playing out of position against Minni, who has a massive stack and has been aggressive, with such a weak hand is generally a losing play. I will fold and wait for a better opportunity.

In the big blind facing a limp from the button (Minni), the pot is 250 and I've already invested 100 with excellent implied odds to see a flop for free. 8s4h is a weak offsuit hand with limited playability, but checking allows me to realize equity cheaply or bluff postflop if needed. Raising a trash hand like this risks being iso-raised by a strong range or playing OOP postflop bloated pot with a weak holding. Minni has shown aggression historically but limped here, so checking exploits position and pot odds while preserving stack for better spots over the remaining 47 hands.

On this Ah7cQs flop, 8s4h is a complete air hand with no pair, no straight or flush draw (only backdoor spade and heart potential). OOP as BB after checking pre-flop to a button limp, betting would be a bluff into a wide button range that includes Ax, Qx, draws, and floats. Pot control is key; checking allows a cheap fold if Minni bets (likely, given her history of post-flop aggression like in hands 52, 76, etc.), or a free turn if she checks back her weak holdings. Preserving stack for better spots in the final 47 hands outweighs bluffing equity here against a positioned opponent with 19k stack. Historical data shows Minni bets flops often when checked to.

I have 2♣3♥ on A♥-7♣-Q♠. This is a complete miss with essentially zero showdown value and no realistic draw (only a double-runner wheel requiring perfect turn+river). Elon checked, which could be weakness or slowplay, but the Ace on board heavily favours his range. Best line is pot control and folding to aggression — don’t invest chips with nothing. I’ll check and fold to any meaningful bet; if he checks back I’ll reevaluate the turn and only continue if I pick up real equity or a very favorable texture to bluff on later.

On the turn, the 4c pairs my 4h, giving me bottom pair with an 8 kicker (84o). The board is A-7-Q-4 with two clubs, introducing flush draws. Pre-flop, Minni limped button (wide range: Ax, Qx, suited connectors, pairs), I checked BB, and she checked flop behind, suggesting medium strength, traps, or weakness rather than a strong betting hand. OOP with bottom pair, vulnerable to Ax/Qx domination and draws, pot control is priority in this tiny 250 pot. Betting thin value risks folds from worse or calls/raises from better; checking induces bluffs from her aggressive history (e.g., hands 52,76,87), allows cheap showdown, or free river. Preserves my 11k stack for better spots with 47 hands left, exploiting her position while realizing equity safely.

Check. On A♥-7♣-Q♠-4♣ with 2♣3♥ I have no pair and only four river outs (any 5) to make the wheel (~8–9% equity). Elon checked flop and turn — that can be either weakness or a slow-played Ax/Qx — but given my hand’s near-zero showdown value and very limited fold equity, I should not invest chips here. Checking preserves stack and keeps the pot small; I’ll fold to any meaningful bet now and only continue if I actually hit the straight on the river (or face a very thin river spot where a small bluff makes sense).

On the river, the board is A♥7♣Q♠4♣A♠, pairing the aces and completing club flush draws and spade flush draws (though I have a spade). I hold 4♥8♠ for bottom pair with an 8 kicker, improved from the turn but still vulnerable OOP against Minni's wide button limping range that checked flop and turn (likely medium pairs like 88-JJ blocked by board, Qx, missed draws like KJs, JT suited, or air/floats). The tiny 250 pot and double-checked streets suggest her range is polarized: traps with AA/AQ (unlikely given limp-check), weaker showdown value, or bluffs/draws that missed. Betting thin value risks folds from worse (e.g., Qx, 88, draws) and calls/raises from better (top pair good kicker, two pair, sets). Raising isn't applicable. Calling N/A. Folding impossible. Checking OOP realizes equity safely for cheap showdown (she may check back worse or weaker aces/Qx), induces bluffs if she bets (her aggressive history: hands 52,76,87,91 where she fired rivers), and preserves my 11k stack for superior spots in the final 47 hands. No fold equity needed in unraised 250 pot; pot control prioritizes long-term stack maximization over marginal bluff/value spots.

I have 2♣3♥ on A♥-7♣-Q♠-4♣-A♠ — I never connected (no pair, no flush, missed the wheel). Elon checked flop, turn and river; that line is consistent with either air or a slow‑played Ax, and with two Aces on board an opponent holding an Ace would have trips. The pot is only 250 and I have essentially zero showdown value or fold equity, so bluffing here is low EV (he will often call small bluffs or has a value hand). I’ll check and preserve my stack; if Elon bets I’ll fold to meaningful aggression.

I am on the button with K4o. The action is folded to me. While K4o is not a premium hand, being on the button gives me a positional advantage. A standard opening raise would be to 300 to steal the blinds or take control of the pot. However, considering the hand history, Minni and Elon have been quite active and often defend. Given I am 100 hands in and have a healthy stack, I will raise to 300 to assert pressure, representing a wider button opening range.

Q℃ 5♦ is a trash hand: offsuit, disconnected, low equity against a button raise range which is wide but still crushes this. Out of position in SB, no reason to defend this speculative hand with poor playability postflop. Pot odds are about 450:250 (1.8:1), but equity too low (~30% vs random, less vs polarized raise). Standard fold to preserve stack for better spots. Long-term EV negative to call or raise.

Flash opened to 300 from the button. I’m in the big blind with 8♥9♣ (offsuit). This is a standard defend — 8-9o has decent equity versus a wide button opening and the call is cheap. Calling 200 (to 300 total) gives ~31% break-even on the pot (200 into 450) and preserves my stack and postflop flexibility. I’m out of position, so I don’t want to inflate the pot with an OOP min‑3bet that won’t fold many hands; a larger 3bet would commit too much with a marginal offsuit hand. Plan: see the flop, continue with any pair/two‑pair or strong draws (open‑enders, pair+draw), pot‑control or fold to heavy pressure when completely missed.

I’m on the flop with 9♣8♥ on A♠4♣3♣ and I completely missed: no pair, only one club (no flush draw) and no meaningful straight outs. The button’s preflop raising range contains a lot of Aces and club combos, so this is a very good c-bet spot for him and the board heavily favors his range. Out of position with essentially zero equity, I want to keep the pot small and not bluff into a single opponent without equity. Plan: check and fold to most reasonable bets; I may occasionally call a very small c-bet (a ~1/3 pot float) to see a turn if the sizing and player tendencies justify it, but I will not lead or check-raise here with this hand.

On this Ace-high flop, I have middle pair (fours) with a King kicker. As the pre-flop aggressor, an Ace-high board is very favorable for my perceived range. Minni checked to me, which is standard. I will make a continuation bet of 325 (half pot) to represent the Ace and potentially take the pot down immediately. If called, I have some showdown value with my pair and can evaluate the turn.

I completely missed the flop (9♣8♥ on A♠4♣3♣). Flash (button) opened pre and now c-bet 325 into a 975 pot — I must call 325. Break-even equity on this call is 325/(975+325) = 25%. My chance to make at least a pair (8 or 9) by the river is ~24% and even those pairs will frequently be behind Ax or better; my backdoor club/runner-runner combos are negligible. I’m out of position and Flash’s button opening range contains lots of Aces and clubs that connect well with this board, so continuing OOP with essentially zero realized equity is negative EV. A tiny c-bet is tempting to float, but the math and table dynamics favor folding and preserving my stack for better spots.

Preflop on the button with Th7s offsuit, a weak speculative hand with poor playability and low equity against typical calling or raising ranges. Pot is 150, to call 100 gives about 2.5:1 odds, but position doesn't justify entering with this hand against deep-stacked opponents like Minni and Flash who have shown aggression. Folding preserves stack for better spots in this long session.

I have A♥5♥ in the small blind with only the big blind to act. Suited ace is a hand I want to play proactively — limp/calling in SB surrenders initiative and gives a positional disadvantage postflop. I’ll raise to 3x the big blind (300 total) to isolate the BB, get fold equity, and price out some speculative hands. This sizing keeps pot manageable OOP but gives me initiative: if Flash folds I pick up the 150; if he calls I’ll c-bet many dry/heart-favorable flops and proceed cautiously on A‑heavy or highly connected boards. If he 3‑bets big I’ll generally fold A5s out of position, but I’ll reassess smaller 3‑bets for a possible defend given block and suited equity.

I am holding Ad Kd in the Big Blind, which is a premium starting hand in a 3-handed game. Minni raised from the Small Blind to 300. Given the strength of my hand and Minni's aggressive tendencies, a 3-bet is the most profitable play to build the pot and take control of the hand. I will raise to 1000 total.

Flash 3‑bet to 1,000 after my 300 open from the SB. I have A♥5♥ — a suited ace with a useful blocker and good backdoor equity. Pot is 1,300 and calling 700 more (committing 1,000 total) makes the pot ~2,000, so I need roughly 35% equity to break even. Flash’s 3‑bet size is moderate and his range likely contains both value (QQ+/AK) and some suited/Ax/connector bluffs, so A5s has reasonable equity and strong implied odds with deep stacks. I don’t want to 4‑bet bluff with a marginal hand OOP (risking a 5‑bet or bloating the pot), and folding gives up too much equity and board realization. I’ll call to see a flop and play the texture: continue aggressively only with hearts/strong improvements or proceed cautiously (check/call or fold to heavy action) on A‑heavy or highly connected boards.

I have A♥5♥ on 3♦ A♠ 5♣ — top two pair. This is a very strong holding on a pretty dry flop with virtually no meaningful draws; the main hands that beat me are sets (AA/55/33) or the unlikely A5 two‑pair that ties. Flash 3‑bet preflop, so his range contains a lot of Ax and premium pairs but also some bluffs; against that range my two pair is usually ahead of single‑Ace hands and most pocket pairs. Out of position I want to extract value and deny free cards that could give him trips, so I’ll lead for value rather than check for deception. Pot is 2,000 — I’ll bet 1,200 (~60% pot): big enough to get called by worse Aces and medium pairs but not overcommit OOP against a potential check‑raise. Plan: if he calls I’ll re‑evaluate the turn and continue for value on safe cards; if he check‑raises a small amount I’ll likely call and proceed cautiously; if he check‑raises massively (polarized to sets/air) I’ll fold/re‑evaluate depending on sizing and pot odds.

I have top pair, top kicker (AK) on a relatively safe A-3-5 board. Minni's lead of 1200 into the 2000 pre-flop pot (a donk bet) is often a sign of a marginal hand like a weaker Ace (AQ, AJ) or a draw (4x) looking to set the price. I want to extract value from those weaker Aces and charge the draws. A raise to 3000 is a strong play that puts pressure on her range while I likely hold the best hand.

I have A♥5♥ on 3♦ A♠ 5♣ — top two pair. My 1,200 lead was value-oriented; Flash’s check‑raise to 3,000 polarizes but his preflop 3‑bet range is heavy in Ax and medium+ pairs. Most of those hands (AK/AQ/QQ/KK/etc.) are behind my two pair; the realistic hands that beat me are sets (33 or 55) or extremely rare quads. Pot is 6,200 and I need to call 1,800 more (i.e. put 3,000 total), so I only need ≈22.5% equity to justify a call. Given the strength of my hand, the blockers I hold and Flash’s shown aggression/bluffing frequency, calling is the correct line — raising would bloat the pot OOP and fold is too exploitable. I’ll call to see the turn and then re‑evaluate (extract value vs Ax/pairs, fold only to clear, polarized action consistent with a set/quads).

Flash 3‑bet preflop and check‑raised the flop, so his range is weighted toward Ax and medium+/bluffy pairs, but he checked the turn — either pot control or a probe to induce a bet. I have A♥5♥ (top two) on a very dry 3♦ A♠ 5♣ 2♦ board; the turn didn’t complete meaningful straights and diamonds aren’t a real threat to my hand (I hold hearts). Pot is 8,000 and I want to extract value from worse Aces (AK/AQ/Ax) and medium pairs while keeping the pot from getting out of hand OOP. A ~50% pot bet is the right balance: it charges worse hands, denies equity, and preserves fold equity if he has a disguised set. I’ll bet 4,000 to get value and control the size of the pot; I’ll re‑evaluate if he responds with a large check‑raise (likely a set) but this sizing is optimal to extract from most of his calling range.

I hold Ad Kd, giving me top pair top kicker, the nut flush draw, and a gutshot straight draw (to the wheel) on an As-5c-3d-2d board. Minni's lead of 4,000 into the turn is a very strong line, suggesting she may have made a straight with a 4 or has two pair. However, with only 5,070 chips remaining and a pot that has grown to 12,000, I am mathematically committed. I have significant equity even against her strongest possible hands (like a straight or sets) due to my nut flush draw and top pair. Given the stack-to-pot ratio, there is no folding here; I must commit the rest of my chips to maximize my potential return if I hit my draws or if my top pair is currently the best hand.

I have A♥5♥ — top two pair on 3♦ A♠ 5♣ 2♦. Flash was the pre‑flop 3‑bettor and check‑raised the flop, so his range is weighted toward Ax, medium/strong pocket pairs and some bluffs. He just shoved/raised my 4,000 turn to 5,070 (effectively all‑in). The pot is 17,070 and I only need to call 1,070 more, so my break‑even equity is tiny (~6%). Very few hands legitimately beat me now (mainly pocket 3s/5s/2s = sets/quads) and a 4 on the river can make a wheel for someone holding a 4 — those are a small number of combos compared to his overall continuing range. Given the price, the strength of my holding, and the likely composition of his range (many Ax and pair+bluff hands that I dominate), this is an easy +EV call. Raising further is either impossible (he's all‑in) or unnecessaryly bloats the pot; folding would be too tight. I will call the 1,070 more (5070 total on the turn).

Button open with Ad3h in a 3-handed pot is a clear raising spot — the Ace blocker and position give good postflop playability and fold equity. Limping is too passive and surrenders initiative; a standard 3x BB open (to 300) is the right size to steal the blinds and define the pot without overcommitting. If I get isolated heads-up I can play postflop aggressively; if I face a large 3-bet from Elon/Flash I’ll fold A3o rather than bloat the pot out of position. Raise to 300 to seize initiative and maximize expected value.

In Hand #106, I am in the Small Blind with QsTd. Minni, on the Button, has raised to 300. While QTo is a decent hand, playing out of position against Minni, who has been active and aggressive, is generally disadvantageous. My stack is quite healthy after the massive win in Hand #105, and there's no need to force a play with a marginal hand out of position. Folding keeps my stack protected and allows me to wait for a better spot to utilize my chip lead.

Preflop in BB facing a button raise to 300 (3BB). My hand QJo offsuit is playable, with decent broadway potential and blockers to strong hands. Minni on button has a wide raising range based on history (frequent raises, plays speculative hands). SB folded, isolating vs button. Pot odds are excellent: calling 200 more into 450 (pot will be 650), ~3.25:1 direct odds, plus implied odds OOP with positionally disadvantaged but multiway potential low. Folding too tight for BB defense strategy vs late position open. 3-bet sizing risky OOP with non-premium hand, better to call and play postflop cautiously, using position to control pot. Stack depths allow (11k effective), aim to realize equity over long game.

Preflop call with QJo offsuit from BB vs button raise was standard defense with position and pot odds, realizing equity postflop. Flop Ks6s7c is very dry for my range but hits villain's wide button opening range hard (Kx, 67, suited connectors, sets). I have complete air: no pair, no draws, overcards blocked by board (no straight draw, backdoor spade but irrelevant OOP). Equity ~20-30% vs her range, but OOP check-fold is optimal to avoid spew. No fold equity to donk bet into aggressor; pot control irrelevant with nothing. Check to induce bluffs or check-behind if weak, ready to fold to bet. Long-term BB defense strategy avoids postflop mistakes with marginal hands on bad boards.

I'm the preflop raiser with Ad3h on K♠6♠7♣. Elon checked to me. I have no pair—just Ace-high—but I have position and the initiative from my preflop raise, and the flop is fairly dry. A continuation bet leverages fold equity, charges any turn/river draws, and can win the pot now against missed hands and medium pairs. The Ace blocker reduces some strong A‑x combinations and my hand has some showdown value if called. I'll size to ~50% pot (325 into 650): large enough to apply pressure and deny correct odds to many floats, but small enough to keep the pot manageable if called. Plan: take it down now if he folds; if called, re-evaluate on the turn and fold to a sizable check‑raise or heavy turn aggression since I have little real equity. Therefore I will bet half‑pot.

Preflop call with QJo from BB vs button raise was reasonable for defense and pot odds. Flop Ks6s7c misses me completely: queen-high air with no pair, no straight draw (missed broadway), no flush draw, minimal backdoor potential. Board favors Minni's button range heavily (Kx, 76/67/65 suited, sets, 89 draw, spade flush draws). Her 325 bet (~1/3 pot) into checked pot shows strength or semi-bluff; my ~20% equity vs betting range isn't enough OOP for a call (3.8:1 odds required ~20% but position and reverse implied odds hurt long-term). No reason to raise (no fold equity with air). Fold preserves stack for better spots; consistent with tight postflop BB strategy after speculative calls.

I am on the button with 7s2s. Although they are suited, this is one of the weakest hands in poker. With only 150 in the pot and needing to put in 100 to enter the hand, the risk-reward ratio doesn't justify playing such a weak hand, especially since I've been playing relatively solid. I will fold and wait for a better spot.

5d2c is one of the worst possible hands, offsuit low cards with no connectivity or straight potential. In SB vs BB, completing adds 50 into 150 pot (3:1 implied odds), but this hand has negligible post-flop playability, low equity against any BB calling range, and poor implied odds even deep-stacked. Folding preserves stack for better spots; no need to defend trash blindly.

Preflop on the button in a 3-handed game with QhKh, a premium suited broadway hand that plays well postflop in position. No action yet, pot 150 with blinds posted. Standard play is to raise for value and to build the pot, taking initiative against loose opponents like Minni and Flash who defend wide. Raising to 3x the BB (300 total) is a good size to get calls from worse hands while keeping pot manageable. My stack is deep enough (11325) to maneuver postflop.

I’m in the small blind with 9♦4♣ — a very weak offsuit hand. Elon opened to 300 (I must call 250 more) and the big blind is still to act, so I’d be out of position and likely multiway. 9‑4o has poor equity vs a button opening range and is a bad candidate for a bluff 3‑bet. With ~105BB effective, there’s no reason to gamble chips in a dominated, low-CE hand — preserve stack and wait for a better spot. Folding is the +EV play here.

I am in the Big Blind holding Ace-Nine offsuit. The Button (Elon) has raised to 300, and the Small Blind has folded. A9o is a strong enough hand to defend against a standard Button opening range. I am getting good pot odds to call (200 to win 450) and will see a flop to evaluate further, keeping the pot manageable and my range wide.

On a flop of Jd-7d-6h, I have Ace-high with an overcard (Ace) and a backdoor straight draw (needs a Ten and an Eight or similar). This board texture heavily favors the pre-flop aggressor's range, which likely includes many broadway hands and pairs. Elon (the Button) raised pre-flop, and I called from the Big Blind. Being out of position, checking to the aggressor is the standard play. I will check and see how Elon responds; if he bets, I'll have to decide whether my Ace-high has enough equity to continue or if I'm better off folding.

On the flop of 6h Jd 7d, holding Qh Kh gives me the nut flush draw (hearts) with two live overcards (K and Q), making this a very strong semi-bluff spot with ~35% equity against a typical calling range. Flash called a preflop raise from the BB and checked, which could represent a weak pair, mid pair, or a weaker draw. With position and initiative from my preflop raise, betting for value, protection against draws (especially diamonds), and fold equity is optimal. Pot is 650; a pot-sized bet (650) pressures Flash while keeping SPR reasonable postflop with deep stacks. Checking risks giving a free card to improve or allowing Flash to rep a hand.

I am facing a pot-sized continuation bet from Elon on a J-high board. While I have Ace-high, the board texture (6h-Jd-7d) is coordinated and favors a range that likely hit a piece or has a strong draw. Calling 650 into a 1300 total pot with only one overcard and no direct draw is too speculative, especially being out of position. I will fold and preserve my stack.

I'm on the button with Ten-Seven of clubs (T7s) — a playable, positionally advantaged hand with suit and connectivity. Pot is 150 and it costs 100 to see the flop; raising to 300 (≈3x BB) is the standard line: it maximizes fold equity, applies pressure to the blinds, and lets me play postflop with initiative and position. Flash (SB) can be looser/aggressive but often folds to pressure; Elon (BB) will defend some hands or 3‑bet occasionally, but my raise size is in line with the table and gives a good chance to win the pot uncontested or realize equity in position if called. Postflop plan: c‑bet favorable textures (clubs, T/7, dry boards), pot control or check back when outdrawn, and fold to large, committed 3‑bets out of position with this hand. Overall raising to 300 maximizes my EV from position and fold equity.

I am in the Small Blind with Queen-Eight offsuit. Minni opened with a 3x raise from the button. While ranges are wider in 3-handed play, Q8o is a weak hand to play out of position against an aggressive opponent. I will fold to preserve my stack and wait for better opportunities.

Preflop in BB facing button raise to 300 after SB folds. Hand is 7s6d offsuit, mediocre at best, poor playability OOP. Pot 450, calling 200 gives ~2.25:1 but equity vs wide button range is insufficient long-term, especially OOP where I'll struggle postflop. Minni aggressive, but 76o not in calling range. Standard fold to preserve stack for better spots.

I am on the button with K7s. This is a strong starting hand in a 3-handed game, especially from late position. I will raise to take control of the pot and potentially take down the blinds immediately. Standard raise sizing here is around 3x the big blind.

3c Jd is a very weak offsuit hand with poor connectivity and low card value. Facing a button raise from Flash, who has been aggressive in late position throughout the history (frequent opens and steals), calling 250 OOP from SB into a 450 pot offers insufficient odds and terrible postflop playability. My hand is easily dominated by Jx, Tx, or any better Broadway, and it struggles against any continuation. Minni in BB may squeeze or call, but no reason to get involved with trash. Folding preserves my stack (11.5k effective) for premium spots in this deep-stacked cash game dynamic, prioritizing long-term EV over speculative defense.

Facing a BTN open to 300 as the BB, calling 200 into a 450 pot gives ~30.8% immediate pot odds. 8h2h is a weak holding but suited and heads-up I should defend a wide range — folding would be too tight, and a light 3-bet is suboptimal with this garbage hand and deep stacks (risk of a 4-bet or expensive postflop street). I'll call to 300 total to see a flop, prepared to fold to heavy aggression on unfavorable runouts but to continue when I hit a pair, two pair, or a meaningful heart draw.

I missed the flop — 8h2h has no pair and only a backdoor heart possibility (need both turn and river to be hearts). I called preflop to defend a wide BB range, but with Q♣ T♥ 4♦ on board and deep stacks I should control the pot. I’ll check and see what the BTN does. Plan: fold to significant C-bet or any raise; I may call a very small C-bet (≈≤1/3 pot ≈200–220) as a cheap float to realize equity, but will give up to larger bets. If checked to, I take the turn free and re-evaluate only if I pick up a pair or a real draw.

I am the pre-flop aggressor on a board that contains two broadway cards (Queen and Ten). This board texture generally favors my range as the button raiser. I have King-high with a backdoor diamond flush draw. By placing a continuation bet, I can often win the pot immediately if Minni has missed, or set up a multi-street play if I improve on the turn. A standard half-pot bet is appropriate here to apply pressure without overcommitting.

I missed the flop with 8♥2♥ — no pair, only a backdoor heart possibility. Flash c-bet 325 into a 975 pot; calling 325 would require ≈25% equity (325/(975+325)=325/1300≈25%). My realistic equity is far lower: 6 immediate pair outs gives ~6/47≈12.8% to hit the turn, the backdoor flush needs both turn+river hearts (~4.2%), and overall multi-street equity vs a BTN c-bet range (lots of Qx/Tx/overcards/value hands) is well under 25%. Per my pre-flop plan I defend wide from the BB but fold to larger c-bets on ugly boards — this is one of those spots. Folding preserves stack and avoids costly turn/river decisions against a wide but aggressive opponent.

Preflop on the button in a 3-handed game with J2o, an offsuit trash hand with poor high card value, connectivity, and playability. No action before me, blinds posted (pot 150, to call 100 for BB). While button position allows wide ranges 3-handed, J2o is too weak—easily dominated by Jx, any pair, or better broadways. Calling gives poor implied odds due to reverse domination risks and poor postflop prospects. Raising is a bluff but risks multiway pots or isolation vs strong BB defense seen in history. Folding preserves stack for better spots; discipline key to long-term EV+ play over 150 hands.

We’re in the small blind with 3♠8♦ — a clearly marginal hand and out of position against the big blind. Pot is 150 and it costs 50 more to complete to a 100-chip BB (i.e. 4:1 immediate pot odds). Raising as a steal with 3‑8o into Flash (who defends and fights back frequently) is thin and invites 3-bets or sticky calls where we’ll be dominated postflop. The low-cost call lets me realize equity heads-up and fold cheaply on most unfavorable flops. Plan: call to see a flop; fold to significant aggression if I don’t improve, continue only with a pair, a strong draw, or a favorable board texture.

I am in the Big Blind with 9h 4c. The Small Blind (Minni) has just limped in. I have the option to check and see a free flop. Given that 9-4 offsuit is a very weak hand, there is no reason to raise here. Checking allows me to potentially hit a favorable board without committing any more chips.

We have bottom pair (3♠) on J♣-9♣-3♦ — heads-up vs the BB who defended preflop and is capable of defending/raising. I’m out of position and this is a vulnerable, marginal made hand; I don’t want to bloat the pot with just a small pair. Checking controls the pot, keeps worse hands and thin bluffs in to extract value, and lets me see a cheap turn to improve or re-evaluate. Plan: check and call a small continuation bet to realize equity, but fold to significant aggression (a large raise) since I’m behind to most Jx/9x and dominated by many hands.

In this limped pot, I have hit middle pair (Nines) with a weak kicker on a J-9-3 board. Minni, the Small Blind, has checked to me. While I have some value, my hand is not strong enough to bet for three streets, and checking back allows me to control the pot size while seeing a turn for free. This approach keeps the pot small and allows me to reassess based on the turn card.

Check. I have a vulnerable bottom pair (3♠) and I’m out of position heads‑up on a J♣‑9♣‑3♦‑7♥ board. Both streets have been checked so far; pot control is the priority with this marginal holding. By checking I realize equity cheaply, keep worse hands and thin bluffs in, and avoid bloating the pot where I’ll usually be behind to Jx/9x or dominated by better pairs. Plan is to call a small turn bet to see the river but fold to significant aggression (big raise).

I have middle pair (Nines) in a limped pot. Minni has checked for the second time on the turn. While I likely have the best hand at the moment, my kicker (Four) is extremely weak, and there are several overcards and draws that could be out there. Betting here doesn't achieve much; if I'm called, I'm often against a better Nine or a Jack that is slow-playing, and if I'm raised, I have to fold. I will check back again to control the pot size and try to reach a cheap showdown.

Board: Jc-9c-3d-7h-4h. I have 3♠8♦ — bottom pair with no meaningful draw. Both streets were checked, indicating weakness or a passive showdown line from the BB. The pot is small (200) and there’s no bet to extract; betting here would be thin value at best and can get called by hands that beat me (Jx/9x) or fold out only the absolute air, so it’s a marginal +EV play at best and needlessly bloats the pot out of position. I’ll take the free showdown and check back to avoid risking chips with a vulnerable hand.

On the river, my hand has improved to two pair (Nines and Fours). Minni has checked on every street after limping in from the small blind, suggesting a weak range that likely contains a single pair or a missed draw. Since I have a very strong hand in the context of this limped pot, I will bet for value, hoping to be called by a worse hand like a Nine, a Seven, or even a smaller pair.

I have a vulnerable bottom pair (3♠) on J♣-9♣-3♦-7♥-4♥ and I’m out of position. Flash checked both streets and now bets 150 into the 200 pot (pot becomes 350); calling 150 would make the final pot 500, so I need ~150/500 = 30% equity to break even. Given the line (check-check then a river value-ish bet) his range is likely weighted to value hands that beat me (Jx, 9x, pocket pairs like 77/44/88, maybe 33) with only a minority of pure bluffs or very small pairs (22) that I actually beat. My equity vs that range is well under the 30% threshold, and my earlier plan was to fold to significant aggression with this marginal hand. Folding preserves my stack and avoids a marginal, high-variance call in a small pot.

I'm on the button with K♦J♠ — a strong, playable hand and the best position. Pot is 150 and it’s folded to me; calling 100 is passive and misses value/fold equity. I open-raise to 300 (standard ~3x BB) to steal the blinds and leverage positional advantage. With a 10,285 stack this size is small risk but gains fold equity; if called I'll C-bet favorable flops and use position to pressure. If facing a large 3-bet, KJo is often a fold; call only vs small 3-bets where pot odds and SPR justify continuing.

I am in the Small Blind with Jd 4s, which is a weak hand. Minni, on the button, has raised to 3x the big blind. Calling out of position with a marginal-at-best hand is generally a losing strategy, especially when I could be squeezed by Elon in the Big Blind. Folding here preserves my stack for better spots.

3d4c is a trash hand: offsuit, low connected cards with minimal potential. Minni's button raise is likely a wide steal attempt, but defending from BB with this hand gives up position post-flop and has poor equity (~30-35% vs wide range, but pot odds require ~31% and implied odds are insufficient for such a weak hand deepstacked). Standard BB defense charts fold offsuit low connectors like this. Preserve stack for better spots.

I am on the button with Ace-Nine offsuit. This is a standard opening hand in a three-handed game. My stack is healthy at 18,140 chips, and both opponents have around 10,000 to 11,000 chips. A 3x raise is standard to apply pressure and build a pot with a strong starting hand.

Preflop in SB with K3o facing a button raise to 300 (to call 250 into 450). K3o is a weak offsuit hand with poor playability and low equity against a typical button opening range, which is wide but still favors folding trash like this from SB. BB acts behind, but no reason to defend this marginal holding. Deep stacks don't change the fold equity math here. Standard fold to preserve stack for better spots.

Facing a standard Button open to 300; pot is 450 and I need 200 more to call (total commitment 300). I have A♣4♥ — a marginal Ace with a weak kicker and I’ll be out of position postflop. Against a button opener this hand is worth defending but not worth a full 3‑bet: a 3‑bet OOP with a weak kicker invites difficult decisions and often gets dominated. Calling preserves weaker Ax and suited hands in my range, gives decent immediate pot odds to see a flop, and lets me use my stack depth to realize equity or apply pressure when I connect. Plan: call 200 more (total 300) and proceed cautiously on the flop — continue with top pair/strong draws, fold to heavy aggression when I miss, and look for spots to extract value when appropriate.

Check. I have A♣4♥ on J♣6♣5♠ — only Ace‑high with a weak kicker and no real draw (only a backdoor club possibility). The board is somewhat coordinated and the button’s raising range contains lots of Jx, club draws and hands that either already beat me or will barrel profitably. Betting OOP with a naked A is a mistake: I’d risk bloating the pot and facing tough decisions. I’ll check to control the pot, see what Flash does, and call small‑to‑medium continuation bets to realize equity (and fold to large raises). If the turn pairs or brings an A or a harmless card I can be more aggressive for value; otherwise keep the pot small and concede to heavy pressure.

I hold Ace-high on a board of Jack-Six-Five. As the pre-flop aggressor, I have the range advantage on this board, even though it connects somewhat with a big blind defending range. Minni checked to me, and a continuation bet is standard here to try and take the pot down immediately or to see where I stand. I have one overcard (the Ace) and a backdoor club draw with the Nine of clubs. A bet of roughly half the pot should be sufficient.

I called preflop with A♣4♥ and checked the flop. Board J♣6♣5♠ — I have only Ace‑high but I do carry the A♣ blocker (which reduces opponent Ax and some club‑draw combos). Flash’s bet (325 into a ~975 pot) is a relatively small c‑bet (~1/3 pot) from a button opener whose range is wide. Raising OOP with nothing but an Ace and a weak kicker is too thin and will lead to difficult decisions; folding is too weak because this bet size gives me reasonable pot odds to realize equity and see a turn. I’ll call 325 to keep the pot controllable, realize equity against his wide c‑betting range, and reevaluate on the turn (fold to heavy continued pressure if I miss, extract value/turn aggression if I pair the A or pick up a favorable card).

Board on the turn is J♣ 6♣ 5♠ T♣ and I hold A♣4♥. The turn club is a very constructive card for me: there are now three clubs on the board and with my A♣ I have the nut-flush draw (9 club outs) plus 3 outs to an Ace — about 12 outs ≈ 26% to improve on the river. Flash opened and c‑bet a small amount on the flop, then checked the turn; that line is consistent with a wide range (Jx, pocket pairs, floats and missed draws) or pot‑control with a made hand. Betting a small‑to‑medium size here uses fold equity (and my A♣ blocker) to take down the pot now or play a profitable semi‑bluff if called. I don’t want to bloat the pot OOP with a marginal made hand, so I’ll fire a probe of roughly 35–40% pot. If he folds I win the 1,300 pot; if he calls I still have ~26% to improve and can proceed cautiously on the river. If he raises big I’ll respect that and fold (he’s representing a very strong made hand).

On the turn, the Ten of clubs completes potential flushes. Minni has led out for 500 into a pot of 1300. I hold the Nine of clubs, giving me a flush draw, along with an overcard (Ace). The pot odds are favorable for a call (500 to win 1800, requiring about 22% equity). Minni's lead could represent a made flush, but it could also be a thin value bet with a pair or a semi-bluff with the nut flush draw (Ace of clubs). Calling allows me to realize my equity and see the river without overcommitting in case she is already strong.

The river (8♥) bricks my nut‑flush draw — I missed and only have A‑high. Flash’s line (open, small c‑bet, check on turn then call my turn probe) maps to a range heavy in one‑pair hands (Jx, Txs), some pocket pairs and a small number of turn‑made two‑club flushes. Bluffing here requires a sizable fold frequency (break‑even ≈ b/(2300+b): e.g. ~23% for a ~700 bet, ~30% for a ~1000 bet). Given Flash’s willingness to call the turn and his wide opening range (he’s sticky enough to call rivers), I don’t trust that he folds often enough to make a pure bluff +EV. I also have no showdown value and don’t want to risk bloating the pot OOP. Best line is to check, preserve chips and fold to any large river raise; if he checks back we go to showdown and accept the result.

On the river, the board is Jc 6c 5s Tc 8h. I have Ace-high with a 9c blocker. Minni led out on the turn when the third club hit and then checked the river. This check often indicates a hand that has some showdown value but isn't strong enough to value bet, such as a Jack, a Ten, or a medium pair. Given Minni's history of being sticky and calling down in previous hands (like Hand #74 and Hand #105), attempting a bluff here is high-risk. While my Ace-high is unlikely to win at showdown, checking back preserves my chip lead and prevents me from losing more chips to a hand that is unlikely to fold to a standard-sized bet.

86o is a weak offsuit hand with poor connectivity and low card strength. Even on the button in a 3-handed game, it lacks the equity and playability to open limp or raise. Opponents have deep stacks and show aggression in history. Best to fold preflop and wait for stronger holdings to maximize long-term EV.

I’m on the small blind with 2♣5♥ (2h5c). Pot is 150 and it costs me 50 to call (total 100) — min-raise is to 200. This is a near-bottom hand with almost no playability out of position. Calling gives only ~25% required equity to break even, and any realistic equity vs Flash’s defending range will be diminished by being OOP and his frequent post‑flop aggression. Raising would be a naked bluff with poor fold equity against this opponent and would needlessly commit chips. With ~92bb and plenty of hands left to play, the safe + +EV line is to fold and wait for a better spot in position.

I have A♠3♠ on the button in a 3‑handed pot. Pot is 150 and it costs 100 to see the flop. A3s plays well in position (nut‑flush/backdoor straight potential) but is often dominated by opponents' A‑ranges and both remaining players have shown preflop / postflop aggression. I prefer pot control and the positional advantage — calling keeps the pot manageable, avoids getting 3‑bet into a big pot with a marginal ace, and lets me evaluate the flop before committing more chips. Plan: call 100, use position to c‑bet favourable flops or fold to heavy resistance if I miss.

I am in the Small Blind holding 9h 7c. The button (Minni) has limped into the pot. While the cost to call is only 50 chips and the pot odds are attractive (5:1), 97o is a very weak hand that performs poorly out of position. Additionally, the Big Blind (Elon) remains to act and has shown a history of aggressive pre-flop play. To preserve my chip lead and avoid being put in a difficult spot post-flop with a marginal hand, I will choose to fold.

AQ offsuit is a premium hand from the big blind facing a button limp after SB folds. Minni's limp from the button suggests a wide range, including speculative hands, suited connectors, or traps, but I have position postflop and a strong ace-high hand that plays well multiway or heads-up. Raising builds the pot, takes initiative, denies equity to weaker hands, and isolates against the limper. Standard raise size of 4x the BB (400 total) is appropriate given the pot size of 250 and deep effective stacks (minni ~9k). Checking would invite multiway pots which is suboptimal with this strong holding.

I already limped and Elon raised to 400. A♠3♠ is worth defending in position heads‑up: nut‑flush/backdoor straight potential, useful blocker to Ax, and reasonable equity vs a typical opener. Calling keeps the pot manageable, preserves position, and avoids getting bluffed off by a stronger Ace if I 3‑bet. Pot odds are attractive (300 to call into a 550 pot), and I can use positional advantage to c‑bet favorable flops (spades, low boards) or fold to heavy aggression on Ace‑high boards where I'm likely dominated. Therefore I will call to 400 total (300 more).

Preflop, I raised AQ offsuit from BB after Minni's button limp, which she called. Flop 5s-4h-2d is a dry, low board that misses most of her limp-call range (suited connectors, small pairs, Ax weak). My ace-high is likely best, with two overcards for equity. As preflop raiser acting first, standard continuation bet to take it down, get value from weaker hands/draws, and protect against overcards or gutshots. Board uncoordinated, no strong draws, so half-pot bet sizes well. Betting 425 (~half pot) balances value/protection/fold equity without overbetting. Her stack 8860 allows room for further play; position on her postflop.

I have A♠3♠ on 5♠-4♥-2♦ heads‑up. This is a keeper: Ace blocker to many Ax combos, plus backdoor nut‑spade/backdoor straight equity. Elon is a preflop raiser and c‑betting a wide range on a dry low board, so my Ace‑high has decent equity and I get good pot odds. Calling 425 into the current pot is a small fraction of my stack and keeps the pot under control while preserving position to act on the turn. Raising would be too polarizing with only Ace‑high; folding surrenders too much equity and gives up the initiative. I’ll call and re‑evaluate based on the turn (raise/lead if I pick up a spade or pair, fold to extreme aggression if I completely miss and he barrels).

Turn 8s adds a spade flush draw to the board (two spades now: 5s, 8s), making it much drawier despite the initially dry flop. My AQ (Ac Qd) remains ace-high with no pair, no flush draw, and only backdoor straight potential. As the preflop raiser who c-bet flop and got called, my range is perceived as strong (overpairs, sets, strong Ax), but her limp-call range (wide: weak pairs 22-77, suited connectors like 63s, 36s, A5s, etc.) hits this board reasonably (pairs 2-8, wheel draws, now spade draws if suited). The 8s could improve her to pairs or complete draws. Pot is 1700 OOP first to act, stacks deep (mine 10.5k, hers 8.4k). Betting risks raising/folding to her position advantage or value from better hands; half-pot (~850) might get called by worse but doesn't build pot maximally. Checking achieves pot control with marginal made hand, induces bluffs from missed draws/floats, realizes equity for free if checked behind, and allows cheap showdown or hero-call if she bets small/weak. Superior to thin value bet or overbet bluffs given vulnerability and improved texture. Will re-eval river or her bet.

I have A♠3♠ on 5♠-4♥-2♦-8♠. I actually already have the wheel (A-2-3-4-5) — a made straight on the flop — and the turn’s spade adds some draw potential but also makes me more secure since I hold the A♠ (I’d make the nut flush if a spade hits). Very few hands currently beat me (essentially only a 6-high straight from a 3+6 combo), while Elon’s range after raising preflop and betting the flop then checking the turn is wide (pairs, single A’s, worse Ax, and some draws). This is a clear value-bet spot: charge worse pairs and single-A hands, deny a free river to some draws, and keep control of the pot. I’ll size to ~1,000 into the 1,700 pot — large enough to extract value from calling hands but small enough to keep worse hands in. If he raises I’ll re-evaluate for the rare cooler; if he folds I lock up the pot.

Facing a turn bet of 1000 into 1700 after my check on a drawier board (now two spades: 5s-8s). My AcQc is ace-high with an ace blocker, no pair, but solid showdown value and blocks her AA/AK nuts. Minni's button limp-call pre, flop call, and now turn bet after check: her range polarized between value (88-55, wheel like 36/63, 8x suited, spade draws) and bluffs (missed gutshots, Ax weak, floated overcards). History shows Minni aggressive, often bluff-heavy postflop (e.g., Hand 19 river bluff, Hand 74 quad nines but aggressive lines). My check reps some weakness or trap, inviting bluffs from her wide limper range. Folding ace-high OOP is too tight given deep stacks, low SPR post-call (~1.3 for her), and her bluffing tendencies; equity ~35-45% vs her range (per equity calcs vs pairs/draws). Raising risks stacking off light OOP to 88/flush/set; call controls pot, realizes equity cheaply, sets up river hero-fold/call with position advantage lost but blocker strength. Pot post-call 4700, her remaining ~6435 allows river play without overcommitment.

River Ah perfectly pairs my Ac, upgrading to top pair top kicker (AcQd pairing Ah) on a board 5s-4h-2d-8s-Ah. Strong made hand likely ahead of Minni's button limp-call pre vs my raise range: favors weak Ax (worse kicker), mid pairs (5x/4x/8x improved on turn), busted spade draws/gutshots (e.g., 63s had flop gutshot, missed), floats/overcards unimproved. Nuts infrequent: sets (55/44/22 low likelihood from limp-call), spades flush blocked by board/holding, straights (A3 unlikely pre, 36/63 speculative but river doesn't complete new). Preflop wide range + flop call my cbet + turn bet after check polarizes her to value/draws/bluffs; my turn call OOP reps strength. Pot 3700, OOP first, SPR ~2 (her 7435, me 9550). Value bet 2k risks raise-fold to better (2p like 85/58, trips A8/A5 unlikely), minimal fold equity needed. Check maximizes EV: induces bluffs/value from aggressor (history: frequent bets post-check, e.g., Hands 19 river 1500-fold, 87 river bets), pot control OOP, realizes equity cheaply, easy call-down with ace blocker/range advantage. Check-behind possible but her style bets thin.

I already made the wheel (A-2-3-4-5) on the flop with A♠3♠ — that straight is still the best 5‑card hand on the river (the river A♥ pairs the board but does not remove my straight). Very few hands beat me (essentially a 4-8 straight from a 6+7 combo); those combos are rare in a preflop raising range. Elon’s line (raise pre, bet flop, check on the river after calling my turn bet) looks like medium strength / showdown hands (Ax, small pairs like 8/5/4, maybe some missed draws) that will often call a value bet. Checking risks giving a free showdown to river callers and folding value; a modestly large value bet will charge A‑combos and pairs and deny a free card. I want a size that gets called by worse Aces and medium pairs but isn’t so huge it folds them all — roughly 2/3 pot is appropriate. I will bet 2400 into 3700. If he check‑raises big it narrows to the rare combos that beat me; I will call reasonable sized raises because my hand is near‑nut.

River Ah pairs my Ac, giving me top pair top kicker (TPTK) on a board 5s-4h-2d-8s-Ah. This is a very strong hand that beats most of Minni's button limp-call range vs my preflop raise: worse aces (AJ-AT, A5s/A4s with weak kickers or no pair), mid-pairs (88-22, 54s/43s/65s that connected flop/turn), busted spade draws (e.g., 97s, JTs missed), gutshots (e.g., 63s wheel draw missed), and bluffs/floats. Value hands better than TPTK are rare: AA/AK (unlikely limp-call pre vs raise), sets (55/44/22 low frequency from wide limp range), two-pair (A8/A5/A4 unlikely), spade flush (draw was live turn but river Ah doesn't complete spades; she'd likely bomb turn harder if drawing). Straight wheels (A3, 63/36) possible but speculative preflop holdings underrepresented in her calling range. Pot pre-river bet ~3700, her turn bet 1000 (semi-bluff/value?), my call, river check induces from aggressor. Now facing 2400 bet into 3700 (pot 6100, to call 2400 for odds ~2.55:1, effective pot 8500). Her stack 5035 post-bet leaves SPR ~1.4 if call. Calling realizes equity vs polarized range (~60-70% equity vs value/bluffs per range estimation), controls pot OOP (button acts last but river), leverages ace blocker, and extracts from thin value/overbets while beating bluffs (Minni history: aggressive rivers e.g., Hand 74 quads but often bluffs like Hand 19/87). Raising min 4800 risks getting called by nuts/set-mining or shoved on by flush/straight while folding out weaker hands; over-raise stacks off light. Folding TPTK too weak given history/live reads (Minni bluff-heavy post-oop checks). Call maximizes EV long-term in deep-stack game.

I am on the button with 3c 2c, which is one of the weakest suited hands in poker. Although I have a positional advantage, the hand's equity is extremely low, and given the active play from both Elon and Minni in previous hands, a steal attempt is unlikely to be profitable enough to justify the risk. I will fold and wait for a better opportunity.

2s8h is one of the worst possible hands in poker: offsuit, disconnected, low cards with no high card strength or straight/flush potential. In small blind vs big blind, completing the small blind with trash like this is -EV over the long run due to poor equity and reverse implied odds if I hit something marginal. Pot odds of calling 50 into 150 (pot will be 200) are about 3:1, but equity against a calling range is abysmal (~20-30% at best). Folding preserves stack for better spots. Opponents have shown aggression but no need to gamble here with 71bb effective stack.

Preflop on the button with K7s suited, a speculative hand with good playability due to suitedness and king-high blocker. Facing what appears to be a small blind limp (pot 150, to call 100 likely SB completed to 100 or limped 50), standard play is to raise for isolation, position, and initiative. Blinds are loose/aggressive based on history with frequent big pots. Raising to 300 (3x the BB) balances fold equity against weak limpers/callers while keeping pot manageable with deep stacks (71bb effective). Calling is fine multiway but raising builds pot OOP when ahead of limping ranges. Folding is too tight in position.

I have A♣5♠ in the SB facing a 3bb open to 300 from the button (pot 450, to call 250). A5o is marginal to call and play OOP, but versus a wide button opening range in 3-handed play it’s a reasonable candidate for a 3‑bet to steal/isolate. I want a sizing that generates real fold equity and discourages the big blind from coming along for free, so I’ll 3‑bet to ~3x the open (900). This sizing folds out a lot of weaker hands and gives me initiative; if Elon folds I pick up the pot, if he calls I still have deep stacks to play postflop, and if he 4‑bets I can fold without a big loss since I’m likely dominated. Action: raise to 900 total.

I am holding Ten-Deuce offsuit in the Big Blind. The action began with a raise to 300 from Elon (Button) and a 3-bet to 900 from Minni (Small Blind). My hand is extremely weak and does not play well against the ranges of a Button raiser and a Small Blind 3-better. Given the strength shown by both opponents and my lack of position/hand quality, folding is the only viable strategic option.

Preflop on the button with K7s facing a 3-bet to 900 from SB Minni after my open to 300. Pot is 1300, to call 600 more (total 900 committed). K7s is a suited king with good blockers (blocks AK/KQ), playability for straights/flushes, and position. SB 3-bet ranges vs BTN open are often wide including Ax suited, broadway, pairs, and bluffs due to position disadvantage postflop. History shows Minni aggressive (frequent raises, big pots like hand 74, 105), so likely polarized. Folding too tight in position with 3.75:1 direct odds + deep implied odds (~60bb effective). 4-betting risks too much stack (min to 1500, pot-committing range bad with this hand). Calling best: control pot, realize equity postflop in position, exploit Minni's aggression by floating/raising draws. Maximizes EV long-term.

Flop is 7♦ T♠ 7♥. I have A♣5♠ — I completely missed and only have one overcard (an Ace) plus a backdoor spade. I was the preflop aggressor, but this is a paired board that disproportionately helps Elon's calling range (pocket pairs, tens, some broadways that still have showdown value). A continuation bet here would have very thin fold equity — many hands that called my 3‑bet (99–QQ, TT, 77, A10/AJ/AK, KQ, suited connectors) either beat me or will call a modest c‑bet, and a raise puts me in an awkward fold vs a big sizing OOP. I prefer pot control: check and evaluate Elon's reaction. If he checks back I see a cheap turn; if he fires a small probe I may call occasionally with the Ace blocker, but I will fold to meaningful pressure (raise or large turn bet). Checking minimizes the chance of bloating the pot out of position with virtually no equity.

Monster top set (trips 7s with Kd kicker) on paired board 7d Ts 7h. Opponent 3-bet preflop from SB (aggressive history: frequent large bets, big pots like #74, #105) but checked flop, range includes missed Ax broadways (AK/AQ/AJ), overpairs (JJ+/QQ), bluffs/floats, some T7s/set-mining.
