Beats vast majority; loses only to quads (77 ~2.5% pre), boat (TT rare in 3bet). IP advantage, bet for thin value/protection vs straight draws (J98,89x), any flush draws unlikely. Half-pot or 2/3 induces calls from worse (Ax, mid-pairs); pot too big risks fold. Size 1200 (~65% pot) optimal balance, builds pot OOP for turns/rivers. Check risks free card to OESD/GDSD or check-raise shove from nuts, suboptimal EV.

I checked the flop for pot control — A♣5♠ missed completely on 7♦ T♠ 7♥ and I’m out of position. Elon’s 1,200 is a relatively small probe (~30–40% of the pot) so it gives good pot odds to continue and will keep weaker hands and bluffs in. Raising OOP as a bluff is unattractive here: I have no real equity and many hands that called my 3‑bet (TT–QQ, 77, pocket pairs, some broadways) will happily continue or put me to tough decisions. Calling preserves pot control, uses my Ace blocker, and lets me reevaluate on the turn — I’ll fold to significant turn aggression unless I pair up or pick up clear equity. Action: call 1,200.

Check. I have A♣5♠ — no pair and no meaningful draw; the 4♠ on the turn doesn’t improve me. Elon called my 3‑bet preflop and barreled the flop for 1,200, which narrows his range toward real pairs/overpairs (77, TT, 88–QQ, A10, etc.) plus some floated broadways. Betting OOP as a pure bluff is unlikely to fold enough of that range and will put me in a tough spot if he calls or raises. The pot (~4,300) vs his effective stack (~5,000) means any large turn bet commits a lot of chips with almost no equity. I’ll keep the pot controlled, check to see the river, and only continue to showdown or call small river bets with the Ace‑kicker; fold to significant river aggression.

Top set on a paired board with added spade flush draw possible on turn. Minni's line (3-bet pre, check-call flop, check turn) suggests a wide range including overpairs (88-JJ, QQ+), top pair (ATs, KTs), two-pair combos (T7 unlikely), draws (spade flush draws, gutshots like 89, J9), and some bluffs/Ax. My hand crushes most of this; only loses to boats (77, T7) or quads (negligible). Position advantage, deep effective stacks (~30bb for me). Pot 4300, betting ~2/3 pot (2800) extracts max value from worse (TPTK, pairs, draws) while protecting vs. draws realizing equity. Smaller bet risks cheap river card; larger risks fold-out. Allows river shove if called. Optimal EV play given villain's aggressive history and tendency to call big pots (e.g., #74, #105).

I’m on the SB with A♣5♠. I 3‑bet preflop to isolate and got called; on the flop (7♦ T♠ 7♥) I checked and called Elon's 1,200 probe. The turn (4♠) didn’t improve me — I have only Ace‑high and, crucially, no realistic draw (there are only two spades out there and I hold one, so I cannot make a five‑card spade flush by the river). Elon’s line — calling my 3‑bet, barreling the flop, then firing a large bet (2,800 into a 7,100 pot) — is heavily weighted toward value (sets 77, TT, overpairs 88–QQ, some T/Two‑pair combos, maybe A10/A7), with relatively few bluffs. Calling 2,800 to win ~9,900 requires ~28% equity; A5o has far less equity versus that range. Raising would bloat a pot I’m almost certainly behind in and is an unprofitable bluff here. Preserve stack for better spots and fold.

Button with J6o in a three-handed pot — this is a standard steal spot. The table's preflop sizing norm is ~300, so I want a size that provides fold equity and keeps ranges honest. J6o is not strong but plays well in position, so I open to 300 to either take the blinds or play a heads-up pot in position. If I face a large 3-bet I’ll fold; if called I’ll use position to c-bet favorable flops or proceed cautiously when checked to.

I am in the Small Blind with 3c 9s, a very weak hand. Minni has raised from the Button to 300. Calling here would put me out of position with a garbage hand against an aggressive opponent. Folding is the standard and most profitable play in this situation.

K4o is a marginal offsuit hand out of position in the big blind facing a button raise to 3bb. Minni's button range is wide, but K4o is easily dominated by Kx with better kickers and plays poorly postflop OOP with reverse position. Pot odds to call 200 into 450 are decent (~31% needed), but equity realization is low due to domination and awkward playability. Folding preserves stack for better opportunities in a long session; no need to defend this trash wide.

I am on the button with K7o. This is generally a hand I want to open with if the action folds to me. However, in this specific 3-handed structure, I'm the first to act pre-flop. Raising with a King-high hand on the button is a standard aggressive play to steal the blinds or take control of the pot. Minni and Elon have been active, but a standard raise to 300 should be sufficient.

3h9d is a trash hand: offsuit, gapped, no straight or flush potential, poor playability postflop. Facing a button raise from Flash, who has been aggressive but likely has a wide range. However, my hand has very low equity against even a loose button opening range (~20-30% tops). Pot odds are 450:250 (~1.8:1, needing ~35% equity), which I don't have. SB defense calls for marginal hands like suited connectors or broadways, not this. Folding preserves stack for better spots in a long session (119/150). No reason to speculate here.

I’m in the big blind with Ac3s facing a button open to 300 (3x). Pot is 450 and I need to call 200 more (300 total), so the required equity is ~200/(450+200)=30.8%. A3o has reasonable heads-up equity versus a standard button opening range and plays well postflop (can hit top pair or backdoor draws). Stacks are deep, but I’m out of position — I’d rather defend cheaply than 3‑bet as a light bluff with a marginal hand. Plan: call to see the flop, proceed on A/3 or strong draw boards, fold to heavy pressure when I miss.

Check. I have A♣3♠ on K♠ Q♦ 6♠ — no pair, only Ace‑high and a runner‑runner spade pull (needs both turn+river, very low equity). Villain opened from the button and will c‑bet a high frequency, but I’m out of position and don’t want to bloat the pot with a marginal hand. Plan: pot‑control — check and fold to meaningful pressure; call only very small C‑bets to realize showdown value. Concretely: if Flash bets ~<=1/3 pot (≈200–225) I will consider a call; fold to ~1/2 pot+ bets or any raise. If he checks back I take a free turn and re‑evaluate on an Ace, a 3, or a spade.

I have flopped top pair with a weak kicker (7) on a relatively wet board (6s Ks Qd). My opponent, Minni, has checked to me after calling from the big blind pre-flop. Her range in the big blind is wide, but I am ahead of many hands like Qx, JT (straight draw), flush draws, and mid-pairs. I should place a continuation bet here to extract value from those weaker hands and to protect my hand against various draws. A standard bet of approximately half the pot (325) is appropriate to maintain pressure and gather information.

I’m out of position with A♣3♠ on K♠ Q♦ 6♠ — no pair and only a very weak backdoor spade possibility. Flash’s bet of 325 is roughly a half‑pot c‑bet (pot now 975; calling 325 would need ~25% equity to break even). Versus a BB vs BTN c‑bet range (lots of Kx/Qx and broadway/value hands) my hand has well under that required equity and almost no fold equity OOP. My preflop plan was to defend only to very small c‑bets (~≤1/3 pot) and fold to larger pressure. With deep stacks I’m preserving chips for better spots, so I fold here.

3-handed on the button with excellent position. Td6s is offsuit but has straight potential and backdoor flush draws. Unopened pot (assuming based on empty log and to-call being BB amount), standard to raise wide from BTN for value/protection/steal blinds. Opponents show tight tendencies in history, folding often to button aggression. Raising to 3x BB (300) is standard sizing for initiative and fold equity. Deep stacks allow postflop playability IP. Folding or calling weakens position.

J♣6♥ is a weak, unsuited hand with very little equity versus a button open. Facing an open to 300 from the button while I’m in the small blind (to call 250) I’d almost certainly be out of position postflop against one or both opponents. Calling 250 into the 450 pot often creates a multiway, dominated spot with poor implied odds. A min‑raise to 500 as a bluff is thin — it’s only a small extra investment that won’t fold out enough of the button’s range and can get me 3‑bet or called by the big blind, leaving me OOP with junk. With a healthy 11k stack and many hands left, preserving chips and waiting for a better spot is the +EV play here. Fold.

I am in the big blind holding 95 suited. Elon raised from the button to 300. With 200 more to call into a pot of 450, I am getting 3.25:1 pot odds. 95 suited is a reasonable hand to defend the big blind against a wide button opening range. I will call to see the flop.

I have flopped two pair (Nines and Fives) on a very dry 3-5-9 rainbow board. This is an exceptionally strong hand in a blind-versus-button situation. Standard procedure is to check to the pre-flop aggressor to allow him to continuation bet with his wider range. If Elon bets, I intend to check-raise to extract value from his top pairs, overpairs, or gutshot draws while building a pot.

Preflop I raised from the button with Td6s, a wide range play for position and initiative. Flash defended from BB and checked flop, putting initiative back to me. Flop 3s5c9h is rainbow, somewhat disconnected, favors my raising range (overpairs, Ax, broadway, sets like 99/55/33, some draws) over Flash's capping range. My hand has a gutshot straight draw with 7 (3-5-6-7 or 5-6-7-8-9 potential), backdoor spade flush draw (6s), and two overcards for equity realization. Deep stacks, great position. Standard to c-bet ~33-50% pot for thin value, fold equity against weak Ax, floats, or missed draws in defender's range. Flash folds often to postflop aggression from history (e.g., Hand 1, 41, 54). Betting 300 (about 45% pot) balances range, denies equity, builds pot when ahead. Checking risks giving free card to draws like 67, J8, etc.

I have flopped top two pair on a very dry board (9h 5c 3s) in a heads-up pot against the button (Elon). This is a premium hand in this scenario. Elon has made a continuation bet of 300 into a 650 pot. My goal is to build the pot and extract value from Elon's range, which includes overpairs (TT-AA), top pairs (A9, K9, Q9), and potentially some straight draws (64, 76). Since the board is rainbow and relatively safe, I will check-raise to 900. This amount is large enough to build the pot effectively while still potentially being called by hands like Ace-high or gutshots if Elon is feeling sticky, or definitely by any pair.

Preflop BTN raise with Td6s was standard wide for position and initiative. Flash flatted BB, checked flop, I c-bet 300 for thin value/protection as board connects decently with my range and denies equity. Flash check-raises to 900 (adding 900, I need 600 more into 1850 pot). This is a strong line from BB defender; range polarized to strong value like sets (33/55/99), top pair good kicker (A9/K9/Q9/JT suited), combo draws (78, T7s for OESD), two pairs (e.g., 93s, 53s rare), fewer bluffs given sizing and history of Flash's postflop strength (e.g., Hand 6 big pots, Hand 74 quads?). My hand: 10-high, backdoor spade flush draw (3s on board blocks somewhat), no made hand, weak gutshots on specific turns (e.g., 4x for 3-4-5-6 gut to 2/7, but still weak), ~10-15% equity vs value-heavy range, ~12-18% overall estimating 40% value/60% bluffs conservatively. Pot odds ~20% equity needed (600/(1850+1200)=19.7%), close but marginal given reverse implied odds (worse if called, rarely ahead), dead money invested minimal relative to risk. Raising bluff bad: poor blockers (no 9/A block), low fold equity vs strong range, commits more OOP-ish (Flash acts first postflop). Position helps but turn check/raise likely. Deep stacks but late in 150-hand game (hand 120), preserve 8550 stack vs aggressive opponents. History shows folding to postflop aggression often correct (e.g., similar spots). Best EV: fold, wait for better spots.

Three-handed on the button with Kc8h and deep stacks (~113BB). K8o is a playable, positionally strong hand — I want to take the initiative and try to steal the blinds rather than just limp. The min-raise to 200 is too small (it invites 3-bets and cheap multiway pots); opening to a standard 3x (300) is the right balance here. Plan: if I get one caller I’ll play aggressively in position (c-bet on favorable, non-wet boards; check or pot-control on heavy Ace/paired/wet boards). If I face a large 3-bet (particularly from deep-stacked Flash in SB) I’ll fold; I’ll consider calling small 3-bets depending on sizing and pot odds, but K8o is not a hand I want to get stacked with out of position. Raise to 300 to seize initiative and exploit position.

I am in the Small Blind with Ace-Queen offsuit, which is a very strong hand three-handed. Minni has raised to 300 from the Button. My options are calling or 3-betting. Given the stack sizes and the need to play a strong hand out of position, I will 3-bet to 1000 to take control of the pot and potentially end the hand pre-flop or isolate Minni.

9h3h is a speculative suited hand but very weak, especially as a gapped connector with low cards. Facing a button open-raise to 300 and a 3-bet to 1000 from the Small Blind (Flash), who likely has a strong range. Calling 900 into a 1400 pot commits too much with poor equity against tight 3-bet ranges and no fold equity. My stack is 8450 (84BB), but folding preserves chips for better spots. Standard fold preflop.

Flash (SB) 3-bet to 1,000 after my button open to 300. Pot is 1,400 and it costs me 700 to call, so I need ~33% equity to break even. Kc8h is a marginal, often-dominated hand vs a typical SB 3-bet range (AK/AQ, broadway Ks, and pocket pairs) and has a poor kicker vs the value portion of that range. Flash has been aggressive and the sizing looks polarized — not a spot I want to get stacked in with K8o. I also can't credibly 4-bet for value and a bluff 4-bet with K8o is thin. Calling to play post-flop gives me inadequate equity and creates difficult post-flop decisions despite positional advantage. Preserve stack and wait for a better spot — fold.

On the button in a 3-handed game, I have a wide opening range. Nine-eight offsuit is a standard opening hand here to put pressure on the blinds, especially as the chip leader. I will raise to the standard 3x blind amount. If I face significant resistance (a 3-bet), I can easily fold this marginal hand.

4c2s is one of the worst possible hands: low, uncoordinated, offsuit, minimal straight potential (only gutshots at best), no flush draws. Facing a button raise from Flash, who is aggressive but still has position and initiative. I'm OOP in SB, pot odds are 450:250 (~1.8:1), but my equity vs a wide button range is poor (~20-30% at best), and playability postflop is awful. No reason to call or raise. Preserve stack for better spots near endgame.

Flash opened to 300. Pot is 450 and I face 200 more to call (300 total). I have T♦6♥ — a marginal hand out of position, but the open size is small and the Button’s range here is fairly wide. Calling gives me ~30.8% break-even equity (200 into a 650 pot), which T6o can meet vs a wide opening range. I won’t 3-bet — this hand has poor blocker/value for a bluff 3-bet and I don’t want to build a big OOP pot. Plan: flat-call to see a flop, play cautiously OOP (check-fold to sizable c-bets on dry boards; continue with pairs, two-pair/sets, or strong draws). This defends my BB frequency without overcommitting.

Check. I have T♦6♥ on 4♠ Q♣ 3♠ — no pair, no spade draw and I’m out of position. I flat-called preflop to defend the BB but don’t want to build a big pot with a marginal hand on a dry, overcard-heavy board. Checking controls the pot, lets me fold cheaply to a sizeable c-bet, and preserves stack for better spots. If Flash makes a small probe I can call depending on sizing for pot odds; I’ll fold to a large, polarizing barrel. If he checks back I can consider a delayed c-bet on a safe turn card.

I am on the button with 9-8 offsuit. I raised pre-flop and was called by Minni in the big blind. The flop is Q-4-3 with two spades. Minni has checked to me. While I have completely missed this flop, as the pre-flop aggressor, this is a good board to continuation bet (c-bet). A Q-high board favors my range as the raiser, and a small bet can often take the pot down immediately if Minni doesn't have a Queen, a pair, or a strong draw. I will bet 325, which is roughly half the pot, to maintain aggression and fold out her weaker holdings.

Flop is 4♠ Q♣ 3♠; I hold T♦6♥ and I’m out of position. I checked to control the pot and see a flop reaction. Flash fires 325 into the pot (pot now 975) — calling 325 would commit me for a price that requires roughly 25% equity (325 into ~1300 total if I call). My hand has no pair and no spade draw (only three clean outs to a ten and some remote runner equity), so versus a button c-bet range weighted toward Qx, broadways, Ax/Kx and value pairs my realized equity is well under the ~25% needed. This is a dry, overcard-heavy board and Flash’s sizing is large enough to make this a clear fold OOP with a marginal holding. I preserve stack and wait for a better spot.

82o is an extremely weak hand with no high card value, no suitedness, and poor connectivity. Facing a raise pre-flop (to call 100 into pot of 150), the pot odds are insufficient for such a speculative hand, especially out of position relative to the raiser. Folding preserves my stack for better spots. Even on the button, this hand has negative equity against typical raising ranges. Prioritizing long-term EV over marginal plays.

Ten-Jack offsuit is a strong, playable hand heads-up vs the big blind — I don’t want to limp from the small blind and give Flash free or cheap flops. I’ll make the minimum raise to 200 to isolate and get value from worse hands while keeping the pot controllable and avoiding overcommitment to a big 3-bet from Flash’s large stack. Preflop: pot is 150 and calling is only 50, but raising to 200 commits an extra 150 (pot ≈400 if called), which is a reasonable risk/reward. If Flash 3-bets large I can fold; if he calls I’ll proceed postflop with standard c-bet/check plans depending on texture.

I have been dealt Pocket Kings (Kh Ks) in the Big Blind. Minni has raised to 200 from the Small Blind. This is a premium hand and a mandatory 3-bet for value. I want to build a pot immediately. Given Minni's raise to 200 (a 2x raise), a standard 3-bet size from the Big Blind would be around 3 to 4 times the initial raise plus the dead money, which is roughly 600-800. I will raise to 700 to maximize value and potentially induce a call or a 4-bet shove from Minni, who has a stack of roughly 10k.

I opened to 200 from the SB with JTs to isolate the BB, but Flash 3-bet to 700 — I must put in 500 more to see a flop (total 700). Pot is ~900, so calling only gets ~36% pot-equity. Against Flash’s large stack and frequent aggression his 3-bet range here is likely weighted toward strong broadways/pairs plus some bluffs; out of position with JTs versus that sizing is a marginal, uncomfortable spot. A 4-bet would be a bluff or commit too much with a hand that plays poorly OOP. Per my preflop plan to avoid overcommitting to big 3-bets, the +EV play is to fold and wait for a better spot with position or a clearer equity edge.

Short-handed (3 players) on the button I want to be stealing frequently. 5♣3♥ is a weak hand, but folding every button hand is too tight in this spot. Limping gives up fold equity and invites squeezes from the aggressive SB/BB. I’ll open with a standard-sized steal to 300 (about 3x the BB) — big enough to have credible fold equity but not commit too much with trash. Plan: fold to any sizable 3‑bet, and if called use position to play straightforwardly (small c-bets on favorable flops, check/call or check-fold dry boards).

I have pocket Nines in the Small Blind. This is a very strong hand in a three-handed game. Minni opened from the Button, which is often a wide range. I want to three-bet here to take the initiative and potentially end the hand pre-flop, or build a pot with a hand that has significant equity. Raising to 1000 is a standard three-bet size out of the blinds.

KJo offsuit from the big blind facing a button raise to 300 and a small blind 3-bet to 1000 is a marginal spot. The pot is 1400, and calling 900 commits nearly 11% of my 8300 stack into a pot where I'll be out of position post-flop against two aggressive opponents with large stacks (Minni 10185, Flash 20115). KJo has decent equity but plays poorly multiway and OOP, especially against ranges that include strong hands like AK, QQ+, or suited connectors from Flash's SB 3-bet range. Raising to 1700 minimum isn't justified without a premium hand. Folding preserves my stack for better spots in this deep-stack cash game, maximizing EV over 150 hands.

I opened to 300 from the button to steal, but Flash (SB) squeezed to 1,000. With 5♣3♥ this is an easy fold. The pot is 1,400 and calling 700 gives me ~33% pot odds (700 / (1,400+700)). 5-3 offsuit has very poor equity vs a typical SB 3-bet range (pairs, broadways, suited A/K, some suited connectors) and offers almost no post‑flop playability or useful blockers. A 4-bet bluff here would be high variance and thinly justified with my stack sizes and villain's sizing. Preserving chips and folding to this sizable 3‑bet is the correct, +EV line and consistent with my preflop plan.

I am on the button with 3h 5c, which is a very weak starting hand. While I have a significant chip lead and could attempt a steal, this hand has very little post-flop equity and both opponents (Elon and Minni) have shown they are willing to defend or 3-bet. There is no need to commit chips with such a marginal holding at this stage of the game.

Flash has folded on the button, so we're heads-up SB vs BB. My 5d8d is a suited connector with good playability and potential for straights/flushes. In SB vs BB, the BB defends wide, but I have position advantage postflop if I raise and get called, plus fold equity to steal the 150 pot. Standard play is to raise speculative hands like this for value and initiative OOP. Raising to 3x BB (300 total) balances my range. Folding is too tight with dead money; limping is weak and invites raises.

I have A♣5♥ in the big blind facing Elon’s small‑blind open to 300. The pot is 400 and it costs me 200 to call, so I’m getting ~2:1 pot odds (need ~33% equity). Heads‑up an Ace has good raw equity vs a standard SB opening range and I’ll have position going into the flop, so I prefer to keep the pot controlled and realize equity rather than inflate it with a marginal 3‑bet with a weak kicker. Plan: call to see the flop, extract value if I pair my Ace, and fold or pot‑control against heavy aggression on unfavorable textures.

Preflop I raised 5d8d from SB after button fold, Minni called from BB with a wide range including pairs, suited connectors, Ax, etc. Flop 8s6c6s gives me top pair (8s with 8d), kicker 5d, plus gutshot straight draw (to 7 for 5-6-7-8). Board is paired (6s), with spade flush draw possible. OOP first to act, pot 600, stacks deep (8k eff). Top pair has showdown value against her calling range (worse pairs like 77, A-high floats, draws). Betting for value/protection denies equity to flush/straight draws (e.g., 97s, JTs, spade Ax). Check risks giving free cards or facing check-raise. Bet 2/3 pot (400) balances value from weaker hands and bluffs/floats in her range, maintains initiative OOP. Folding or checking too passive with this strength.

Call 400. Elon was the preflop opener and now makes a small-to-medium c-bet on a paired 8-6-6 flop — a wide continuation range is likely (Ax, broadways, some 8x, medium pockets, occasional bluffs). I have A♣5♥: one overcard and a tiny backdoor club draw. Calling 400 into the ~1000 pot gives me excellent pot odds (I only need ≈22% equity) to see a turn and realize my equity against a wide c-bet range. I’m out of position, so I don’t want to inflate the pot with a marginal raise that will often get called by better hands (8x, 99+, 66) and put me in difficult spots. Plan: call to see the turn; fold to significant turn aggression unless I improve (pair the A or 5, or pick up a club), otherwise proceed cautiously and extract value when I pair up or Elon shows weakness.

Turn board brings the 4c, completing some straight draws (e.g., 57 now has a straight) and adding a club flush draw to the existing spade flush draw on a paired board. I have top pair eights with a weak 5 kicker and still a gutshot (needing 7 for the wheel straight), but my hand is vulnerable to any 6x, 87, 75, better pairs, or now made straights/draws in Minni's flop-calling range. Pot is 1400, I'm OOP first to act with deep effective stacks (~7600). Betting thin value risks getting check-raised by stronger hands or called by hands with equity that outdraw me; it also bloats the pot with a marginal holding unlikely to improve. Checking induces bluffs from her missed draws (e.g., pure spades, gutshots), pots control, realizes equity safely against her range, and allows me to call/raise selectively on river if checked back or bet small. This balances my range OOP after flopping top pair and balances check-raises from monsters. Folding equity isn't needed as OOP; checking extracts max value long-term in this spot.

Elon checked the turn after c-betting the flop. I hold A♣5♥ on 8♠-6♣-6♠-4♣. My original plan was pot-control after calling the flop and to only inflate the pot when I improved or Elon showed clear weakness. The turn actually kills my backdoor club draw (I needed both turn+river clubs on the flop), so I only have 6 clean outs to pair an Ace or a 5 on the river (~6/47 ≈ 13% to improve). Betting here would often get called by hands that already beat me (66/88/8x) and only fold out pure air; it also risks a check-raise from a strong made hand. With ~1.4k in the pot and deep stacks, I prefer to keep the pot small and realize my equity on the river. Check behind, see the river, and then extract value if I pair or fold to strong river aggression otherwise.

River Qd is a brick for most draws; it doesn't complete spades or clubs (still possible flush draws if she has them), adds no straight, and pairs no suited connectors likely in range. Board remains paired 6s, scary for trips/boats (66, 68, 64, 46), two pairs, or now 57 straights (5-6-7-8 using 4c-6-6-8). My top pair 8s (weak 5 kicker) has showdown value against her flop-call/turn-check range: missed spade/club draws (A/K spades, suited connectors like 97s/JTs, Ax suited), weaker pairs (77/99/T7 etc.), or floats. OOP after mutual turn check, pot 1400 with deep stacks (~7600 eff). Betting thin value (e.g., 800-1000) risks check-raise from monsters or calls from equity-heavy hands/draws that beat me; induces raises from 2p+/better 8x. Checking realizes equity safely, controls pot, wins vs bluffs/missed draws she might rep, or showdowns weaker hands. Balances as a bluff-catcher in OOP river range post-check turn. Long-term +EV vs her history of calling stations but folding to aggression when weak (e.g., many folds to river bets). No fold equity needed OOP; maximize by checking.

I have A♣5♥ and did not improve on the river Q♦. Elon c-bet the flop and then checked the turn and river — a line consistent with a wide C-bet range that gave up to the turn or a medium-strength hand trying to pot-control (8x, small pairs, some Ax, occasional bluffs). With only Ace-high, I have very little value to extract and betting will mainly fold pure air while getting called by every hand that beats me (66, 88, 8x, Qx, A6/A8) or get check-raised. Pot is 1,400 and stacks are deep enough that any meaningful river bet creates a bloated pot with a marginal holding. My pre-stated plan was to pot-control and only inflate when I improved — I didn’t — so the +EV play is to check and take my showdown rather than fire a thin bluff or risk a larger loss. Check (amount 0).

6h9d is a weak offsuit hand with a gap, poor playability multiway, and low equity against typical calling ranges from blinds. Even on the button with position postflop, the pot odds (calling 100 into 150) don't justify it given deep stacks and late stage of the game (hand 126/150). Folding preserves my stack for stronger spots where I can realize my edge as a superior player.

Heads-up on the button (small blind acts first preflop but has position postflop). Kc2s is a weak hand, but in heads-up you should open a wide range to take the initiative and use position. A small flat-call gives the BB correct odds and surrenders initiative; a min-open is too small and invites loose calls or 3-bets. I’ll open to a standard 3x BB (300 total) to maximize fold equity, leverage the K blocker, and keep stacks deep for postflop play. Plan: c-bet favorable textures if called; fold to a large, committed 3-bet.

Q5o is a weak hand to defend the Big Blind with against a 3x open from the Small Blind. Although I am getting 3:1 pot odds, the hand performs poorly out of position and is easily dominated. I will fold and wait for a better spot.

Pocket nines on the button facing only the blinds — this is a clear open-raise spot. With ~95BB effective I want to build the pot and isolate the blinds while preserving post‑flop maneuverability, so I’ll raise to 3x the BB (300 total). That sizing gives good fold equity, discourages limp-calling fields, and extracts value from worse pairs and broadways. Plan postflop: continuation bet on many dry flops for value, proceed cautiously on ace/overcard-heavy or highly coordinated boards; call small-to-medium 3-bets for implied odds (set mining) but fold to large 4-bets or jam unless confirmed strong.

I am holding King-Jack of Hearts in the Small Blind. This is a very strong hand in a three-handed game. Minni raised to 300 from the button, which is a standard opening. Given that I am out of position for the rest of the hand, I want to take the initiative or win the pot immediately. A 3-bet (re-raise) is the standard play here to put pressure on the button opener and potentially isolate. I will raise to 1000, which is roughly 3.3x the initial raise.

98s is a suited connector with good playability postflop, strong potential for straights and flushes. Facing a button open and SB 3-bet in a deep-stacked 3-handed pot (effective SPR post-call around 8-9), calling offers excellent implied odds if we hit, while keeping the pot multiway potential if Minni calls. Folding is too tight in BB vs SB isolation attempt; 4-betting risks too much with a bluff that doesn't have great blockers and lower fold equity deep. Pot odds are favorable: calling 900 into 1400 (pot becomes 2300). History shows Flash aggressive but exploitable postflop.

I opened to 300 from the button and Flash (SB) 3‑bet to 1000, Elon (BB) called. Pot is 2300 and I need to put 700 more (1000 total). With pocket 9s in position against an aggressive SB 3‑bet and a calling BB, the correct, exploitative line is to call. Calling preserves our implied odds for set‑mining, keeps the pot manageable and gives me position on both opponents postflop. A small 4‑bet here (to 1700) would commit more chips with a medium pair, remove some of our implied odds and won’t fold out many of Flash’s value hands; a larger 4‑bet is overly polarizing and risky given Elon’s call. I’ll call 1000 total, see a flop in position, and proceed based on board texture: look to extract value on dry boards, pot‑control or fold to large aggression on highly coordinated/overcard boards, and capitalise if I flop a set.

On this flop of Ac-Ts-Ad, I have King-high with a gutshot straight draw (needing a Queen). As the pre-flop 3-better, this board generally favors my range, but since I am facing two opponents who both called my 3-bet, the probability that one of them holds an Ace or a Ten is increased. Betting into two people on a board this static is risky without a made hand. Checking allows me to see how they react and potentially realize my equity for free if it checks around, or evaluate the strength of their bets if they choose to lead. I am out of position against both players, so pot control is a priority here.

98s completely misses the Ac Ts Ad flop: no pair, gutshot straight draw at best (needing 7x for 7-8-9-T), no flush draw despite suited (only Ts spade). Paired aces board favors strong hands like AA, AK, AT, sets. Preflop call was speculative for multiway implied odds, but flop equity <20%. Flash's SB check after 3-bet suggests weakness (air, weak Ax, or trap), but OOP vs button Minni (who called 3-bet cold), betting risks getting raised/folded out by monsters while called by worse. Checking controls pot, realizes equity cheaply, allows turn fold if missed. Bluff EV poor deepstacked with opponents aggressive per history. Prioritize stack preservation with 24 hands left.

Flop is Ac Ts Ad — a very bad texture for pocket 9s. I’m multiway against a SB 3‑bettor (Flash) and a caller (Elon), both of whom have ranges that include Ax and big pairs. With an underpair to the paired aces there’s almost no value to betting and my bluff equity multiway is thin and vulnerable to check‑raises/calls. I want to keep the pot controllable and see a turn for free so I’ll check. Plan: fold to significant aggression (check‑raise or large bet) since I’m almost always behind; consider calling a very small probe if I judge one opponent is trying to buy the pot, otherwise check‑down and reassess on the river.

The board has double-paired (Aces and Tens), meaning any Ace or Ten now has a full house. After checking the flop and seeing both opponents check behind, the strength of everyone's hand remains somewhat ambiguous. I hold King-high, which has very little showdown value if an opponent has a pocket pair or a Ten, and zero value against an Ace. Betting here is unlikely to fold out a better hand (like a pocket pair or a Ten) and will only be called by hands that beat me. Checking allows me to see the river for free and evaluate if the hand goes to showdown cheaply, especially since I am out of position against two players in a 3-bet pot.

Turn pairs the Tens (Ac Ts Ad Tc), further improving opponents' likely made hands (AA, ATx, sets, two-pair combos). 8h9s remains a complete airball: no pair, marginal gutshot straight draw to J (unlikely), no flush draw (only Ts and 9s spades). Preflop speculative call hit nothing, flop checked through multiway. Flash's double check suggests possible weakness or trap, but betting risks raising range (Ax, Tx) or getting called by worse rarely. Checking preserves stack (8000 effective, 24 hands left), takes free river card or folds to bet. Bluff EV negative deepstacked vs aggressive histories (Flash/Minni often barrel value). Prioritize survival and pot control over thin value/semi-bluff with <10% equity.

The turn (Ac Ts Ad Tc) is a nightmare for an underpair. With two players still in — the SB 3‑bettor (Flash) and the BB caller (Elon) — our 99 has virtually no showdown value versus their ranges: any Ace or Ten in either opponent’s hand is now a full house (or quads if they started with pocket Aces/Tens), and many pocket pairs and broadway combos that called/3‑bet preflop beat our kicker. Fold equity is negligible multiway, and our chance to improve to a set on the river is tiny (~4.3%). Betting here would bloat a pot we are a large favorite to lose and will get called by better two‑pair/full‑house hands or check‑raised by value. I’ll exercise pot control: check and see the river. If either opponent bets, I will fold to any substantial aggression and only consider calling a very small, obvious probe. If checked through, reassess on the river (check‑down unless I hit or see a highly exploitable river action).

The board has double-paired (Aces and Tens) and also contains a Jack. My hand is King-Jack, which gives me Two Pair (Aces and Tens) with a King kicker (Ac-Ad-Ts-Tc-Kh). Given that the flop and turn were checked around by all three players, it is unlikely anyone is holding an Ace or a Ten, as they would likely have bet for value by the turn. However, a straight is possible if someone holds KQ (A-K-Q-J-T). Since I am first to act on the river and my hand mostly functions as a bluff catcher or a potential chop (against another King), betting offers little value. Only hands that beat me (an Ace, a Ten, or KQ) or hands that chop with me (a King) are likely to call. Checking allows me to see if an opponent bets and potentially get to showdown cheaply.

River Jc completes club flushes (Ac Tc Jc) and possible straight draws (e.g., QK for broadway, but mainly clubs dangerous). My 8h9s is pure air: 9-high no pair, no straight (8-9-T-J needs Q), no flush, <10% equity vs. opponents' ranges. Preflop speculative call with suited connector missed entirely; checked through all streets multiway indicates capped ranges but favoring Ax pairs+, two-pair (AT/AJ), trips, sets, full houses from Flash's SB 3-bet and Minni's flat. Flash's OOP river check suggests non-nutted hands unwilling to bet into two (possible weak Ax, missed draws), but still ahead of my nothing. Bluffing risks 8000 stack into 3000 pot vs. two players (hard to get both to fold Ax+ OOP/BTN), low fold equity deepstacked (23 hands left, preserve 80bb stack). Checking realizes negligible showdown equity cheaply, induces Minni bet-fold scenarios, prioritizes survival/max EV over high-variance bluff. History shows aggression but checked rivers often weak.

Board is Ac Ts Ad Tc Jc — with pocket 9s I have no realistic showdown value versus the ranges that Flash (SB 3‑bettor) and Elon (flat caller) represent. Both checked flop/turn/river; their preflop lines make Ax/Tx and big pairs common, and any Ace or Ten in their hands now beats me (full house/trips). Betting here is pointless for value (only worse kickers would ever call) and bluff equity is extremely thin multiway. I’ll avoid bloating the pot with a marginal hand: check to see showdown and plan to fold to any meaningful river bet. Action: check (0).

I am on the button with a premium pocket pair (Pocket Tens). This is a strong starting hand that I should raise with to build the pot and gain initiative. Given the table dynamics, a standard 3x open to 300 is appropriate.

A6o is a weak offsuit ace with poor kicker, not strong enough to call or raise a button raise out of position in the small blind. Flash is frequently aggressive from the button, often stealing blinds based on history, but calling OOP risks domination by better aces or draws and commits to tough postflop spots. Folding preserves my stack (7950) for better opportunities with 22 hands remaining. Pot odds are not compelling (calling 250 into 450 pot ~36% equity needed, but actual equity vs range is lower).

I’m in the big blind with Td6d facing a standard button open to 300. I’ve already got 100 in, so calling 200 to make my total 300 gets me into a 650-chip pot — about 30% immediate pot odds. T6s has reasonable equity vs a wide BTN opening range (and good playability with straight/flush potential). Out of position it’s marginal to inflate the pot with a bluff 3-bet (T6d doesn’t block premium holdings), so I prefer to see a flop and play postflop based on texture: fold to heavy action on dry boards, continue aggressively when I hit top pair, strong draws, or disguised two-pair/flushes. If Flash 4-bets preflop I will fold. Therefore I’ll call the 200 and see a flop.

I’m OOP in the big blind with Td6d on A♠ 4♦ 3♠. Pot is 650 and there’s no bet to me. I have no pair and only a runner‑runner diamond flush possibility (I hold two diamonds and the 4♦ on board) — making a flush by river requires both turn and river diamonds (~4%); I also have 3 outs to pair the Ten (~12% to hit by river). The flop heavily favors the button’s opening range (A-x, spade combos, many hands that will c‑bet this texture) and I don’t block A or many of his bluff/medium hands, so leading here is low EV — I’d be bloat the pot OOP with very little equity or fold equity. Optimal line is pot control: check and see how he acts. If he checks back I get a free turn to improve; if he bets small I can call to realize equity and re-evaluate; if he barrels big I can fold and save chips. Checking keeps options open and minimizes risk.

On this Ace-high flop, my pocket tens are a medium-strength hand with decent showdown value. While I was the pre-flop aggressor, betting into Minni's check might force out hands I beat while keeping me in against any Ace in her range. By checking back, I control the size of the pot, hide the strength of my hand, and can evaluate her action on the turn while potentially inducing a bluff from lower pairs or draws.

Turn: I have T♦6♦ on A♠ 4♦ 3♠ 4♣. After the turn my backdoor diamond flush is dead (turn is a club) and I have no pair. I only have the three remaining Tens and three remaining Sixes (≈6 outs, ≈13% to improve to a single pair by the river), and even if I pair I will often be behind against the Button’s likely checking range (A‑x, slowplayed pairs, or medium pairs). Both players checking the flop indicates the Button is either pot‑controlling an Ace or has a medium/weak holding — not necessarily folding to a turn bluff. OOP this is a poor spot to press as a bluff because I have almost no fold equity and I can get raised by hands that already beat me (44, A4, sets, or even A‑x turned into a value line).

Plan: Pot control and give myself a free river card. Check the turn. If Flash bets a very small probe (tiny, <~100) I might call as a cheap realization of my small equity, but I will fold to any meaningful turn bet or a raise (no pair, no backdoor draw). If Flash checks back, I’ll re-evaluate on the river and either take a cheap bluff spot on a favorable card or check/fold to aggression. Preserve stack and avoid bloating the pot OOP with a garbage hand.

On the turn, the board has paired (4c), which reduces the likelihood of Minni having a set. However, the Ace remains the primary concern. Since Minni checked for a second time, it is unlikely she has a strong Ace or a four. My pocket tens still hold significant showdown value against her range, which likely consists of middle pairs, small pairs, or spade draws. By checking back again, I am maintaining pot control and ensuring I get to showdown cheaply while not risking a check-raise on a board where my hand is strictly a bluff-catcher against any Ace.

Check. I missed the river — T♦6♦ has no pair and there’s no flush (only 4♦ and 8♦ on board plus my two diamonds leaves me 4‑card). The final board A♠ 4♦ 3♠ 4♣ 8♦ heavily favors the button’s opening range (many A‑x, some 8x, 4x, and medium pairs) and Flash has shown passive lines by checking flop and turn. I don’t block enough of his value combinations and I have almost zero fold equity, so a river bluff would need an implausibly high fold frequency to be +EV. Pot (650) is small relative to my stack (8,285) so preserving chips for profitable bluffs/values in better spots is the right long‑term play. Check and fold to any meaningful bet.

On this board of A-4-3-4-8, my pocket tens have significant showdown value against Minni's range, which likely consists of mid-to-low pairs or missed spade draws. Since the action has been checked through on the flop and turn, it is highly likely my hand is best. Betting now is unlikely to get called by worse hands, as most hands that would call (like an Ace or a 4) would have likely bet for value themselves by now. Therefore, I will check back to realize my equity and win the pot at showdown.

Preflop on the button with T8s suited connector, a speculative hand with good playability postflop, especially in position. In a 3-handed pot (likely SB limped or both blinds in with pot 150 and to-call 100), raising from the button has high fold equity against the blinds, builds the pot when ahead, and allows stealing the dead money. Minni and Flash have shown aggression but also folds to button raises in history. Standard raise sizing around 3x BB. Raising to 300 total commits a small amount relative to my 7950 stack while maintaining initiative. Folding too tight late in tournament; calling too passive without isolation.

Hand: 4♠9♣ in the small blind facing a button open to 300 (pot 450, 250 to call). This is a very weak, offsuit hand that plays poorly out of position. Calling 250 to see a 3-way pot or to face a big blind action leaves me with little equity and poor implied odds; a squeeze/3-bet with this hand is overly ambitious and unnecessarily bloats the pot out of position. With a healthy stack I want to preserve chips for better spots. Fold and wait for a stronger, positionally favorable hand.

I am in the big blind with 3s 2d, one of the weakest possible hands. Elon, the button, has raised to 300. While I only need to call 200 more to see a flop, my hand has virtually no playability or equity. Given that I have a very large stack (23,665) compared to my opponents (Elon has 7,650 and Minni has 8,235), there is no need to defend with such a poor hand and risk losing more chips out of position. I will fold.

Button with 6s4h, effective stacks ~80+ BB. This is a weak, unsuited hand but I have the best position — standard strategy is to open-raise a wide range from the button to steal the blinds and take the initiative. Min-raise to 200 is too small and invites multiway calls; I’ll size to ~3x the BB to 300 to get reasonable fold equity and make postflop playability in position more profitable. Plan: fold to a sizable 3-bet (6-4o has poor equity vs a 3-bet range); if called, use positional advantage to c-bet favorable flops and proceed cautiously with marginal boards.

I am in the Small Blind with J9o. Minni has opened from the Button to 300. In a three-handed game, this is a standard open range. J9o is a marginal hand that doesn't play particularly well out of position against an aggressive button. Given the stack depths and the fact that calling creates a bloated pot out of position against two players (if the Big Blind calls), the most prudent play here is to fold and wait for a better spot to utilize my chip lead.

KJo offsuit is a strong defending hand from the BB against a button raise, given Minni's frequent raising tendencies observed in the hand history. The pot odds are favorable (200 to call into 450, over 2:1), and the hand has good playability postflop with straight potential, overcard value, and decent equity against a wide button opening range (~35-50% hands). Folding is too tight, 3-betting risks too much OOP with a non-premium hand deep-stacked, so calling to realize equity and control pot size is optimal.

The flop Qc7s2d completely misses my KJo, leaving me with just king-high and a weak backdoor spade flush draw. The board is very dry and rainbow, with no flush draws or strong straight draws developed yet, which favors checking to pot control out of position against Minni's wide button opening range. Her range includes many overcards, broadways, and pocket pairs that also miss, so she may check back weak hands or c-bet light. Betting thin for value or as a bluff OOP risks getting raised off equity or stacking off poorly later; checking realizes my ~30-40% equity against her range more efficiently, keeping the pot small while retaining fold equity on later streets if needed. With deep stacks (7800 into 650), no need for aggression here.

I was the preflop raiser from the button and Elon checked the flop — I have position and a wide continuing range here. The flop Qc‑7s‑2d is very dry and disconnected to many hands; as 6s4h I have no pair and almost no realistic draws, so this is a pure c‑bet bluff. I’ll size to ~1/2 pot (325) to present a believable Qx/7x or a pocket pair and to maximize fold equity versus Elon’s checking range. If he check‑raises large I’ll give it up (I have no showdown value); if he calls I’ll proceed cautiously on the turn and only barrel further with improvement or a clear fold‑inducing card. This size balances fold equity with pot control against a single opponent.

Facing a 325 bet into 975 pot from the button after my check OOP on a dry Qc7s2d flop with KJo (king-high, backdoor spade draw). Pot odds ~3:1 require ~25% equity, but KJo has limited playability OOP vs Minni's aggressive button range (which includes QQ+, Qx, 77+, Ax, KQ/KJ, bluffs). Equity is marginal (~30-35% at best), but calling keeps me committed pot OOP with no strong draw or pair; she'll often c-bet turn/river, forcing tough spots. Raising OOP with weak king-high risks getting called by better (Qx/Ax/pairs) or shoved on. Folding realizes equity correctly preflop/call, avoids bloating pot OOP on missed board, preserves stack for better spots. Minni's history shows frequent agg but this board favors her value range.

I am on the button with K9o, which is a strong starting hand for three-handed play. I have a significant chip lead over both Elon (7750) and Minni (8485). Opening on the button allows me to put pressure on the blinds and take control of the hand. A standard raise to 300 has been effective throughout the session to thin the field or pick up the pot pre-flop.

Preflop in the small blind facing a button raise to 300 (3x BB). My hand is J2o, one of the worst possible starting hands with no connectivity, suitedness, or high cards. Button ranges are wide but still crush this hand. Pot odds are marginal (calling 250 into ~450 pot), but Minni still to act behind, and postflop playability is awful. Standard fold to preserve stack and wait for better spots. Opponents are aggressive but no reason to defend trash here.

Flash opened to 300 from the button; I'm in the big blind with T♠4♦. Pot is 450 and I need to call 200 more (300 total). T4o is a weak, often-dominated hand, but the call is cheap — calling 200 into 450 makes the pot ~650 and requires only ≈30.8% equity to break even. Flash's button opening range is fairly wide heads-up and I should defend the blind rather than folding every marginal hand; 3-betting with T4o is spewy and unnecessary. Plan: call to see a flop, then play straightforwardly OOP — continue only with a ten (top pair) or strong draws, otherwise fold to significant continuation bets. Action: call 300.

I have top pair (T♠) with a very weak 4-kicker on Td Jh 3h. Flash opened wide from the button, so his range is broad and contains a lot of worse tens, broadways and some heart draws. The flop is fairly dry aside from two hearts — I don’t want to bloat the pot OOP with a marginal kicker or thin-value raise into likely Jx/overcards. I’ll check to control the pot and induce c-bets; plan is to call small-to-medium continuation bets (~<=1/2 pot ≈325) for value and fold to very large raises that look like two-pair/sets or huge polarized bluffs. If he checks back I can consider a small delayed-value bet on safe turns. Action: check to keep options and preserve stack.

On this flop (Td Jh 3h), my King-high has two overcards to the board but no direct draw other than a backdoor straight. Minni has checked to me. While I have position and the betting lead from pre-flop, this board texture (broadway cards with a flush draw) connects well with a Big Blind's calling range (hands like QJ, JT, QT, or heart draws). However, as the pre-flop aggressor, a small continuation bet of around half the pot is standard to maintain pressure and potentially get folds from hands that missed completely, like low pairs or lower high cards. If she calls or raises, I can re-evaluate on the turn.

I have top pair (T♠) with a very weak 4-kicker on Td Jh 3h. Flash opened wide from the button, so his flop stab of 325 into a 975 pot (~1/3 pot) is a small c-bet that contains lots of worse tens, broadways and some heart draws/bluffs. Calling 325 requires ~25% equity (325/(975+325)=25%), which my hand has versus a wide c-bet range. Raising OOP with this kicker is spewy — I’ll get called by Jx/Tx that dominate or fold out worse hands, and I don’t want to bloat the pot out of position. Folding is too tight given the pot odds. Plan: call to get value from worse tens and bluffs, then re-evaluate on the turn — fold to large turn barrels on scary cards unless I improve, consider a small value bet or check-call lines on safe turns. Effective stack after the call ~7,960 (SPR ≈6), so proceed cautiously. Action: call 325.

I have top pair (T♠) with a very weak 4-kicker on Td Jh 3h 8s. Pot is 1,300 and my stack ~7,960 (SPR ≈ 6). Flash is a wide button opener who c-bet small on the flop and I called, so his range contains a lot of worse tens, broadways, and some bluffs. The 8♠ is a relatively harmless turn — it doesn’t give many new two‑pair combos or complete the heart draw — so I want pot control. Betting out OOP with a marginal kicker risks folding out worse tens and getting check‑raised by Jx/two‑pair/sets; raising is spewy. Best line is to check and let him bluff or value-bet. Plan: check now; call small‑to‑medium turn bets (~≤1/2 pot ≈650) for value, fold to large polarized raises/overbets unless I pick up a read he’s bluffing, and if he checks back re-evaluate the river for a small value bet or showdown.

I have King-high with a gutshot straight draw (needing a Queen to make 9-T-J-Q-K). After Minni called my flop continuation bet, her range likely consists of a pair (Jacks or Tens) or a heart draw. The 8s on the turn is relatively neutral. Since I have position and some equity with the gutshot and overcard, checking back is the most prudent play to see a free river and potentially improve without risking a check-raise.

Pot is 1,300. I have top pair (T♠) with a very poor 4-kicker. Flash opened wide preflop, c-bet small on the flop and then checked the turn — his line is wide and contains lots of worse Tens, Jx, and missed heart draws. He checked the river as well, which is a good spot to take a small, thin-value stab rather than give a free showdown to worse Tens and some calling Jx combos. I don’t want to bloat the pot or get check‑raised off my weak kicker, so I’ll bet small (~1/3 pot) to extract value and fold out pure bluffs; if he raises I’ll fold to avoid committing with a marginal kicker. Action: small value bet to target worse Tens and thin-callers.

Minni called my pre-flop raise and my flop continuation bet, then checked the turn. Her lead on the river for 400 into a 1300 pot (roughly 30% pot) suggests a marginal value hand like a pair of Jacks or Tens, or potentially a straight if she had a hand like 9-7. Since I have only King-high with no showdown value and my turn check makes it difficult to represent a very strong hand with a bluff-raise, the most prudent action is to fold and move on to the next hand.

83 suited on the button in a limped pot (pot 150, to call 100). While position is good and suited hands have some multiway potential for flush or straight draws, 83 is too gapped and weak overall. Poor playability postflop, low equity against likely calling ranges from SB and BB who have shown aggressive tendencies in history. Better to fold trash and wait for stronger spots with 19 hands left; preserve stack for value opportunities.

In the small blind with 7♦2♦ (the worst starting hand), I’m out of position against Flash (big blind) who has been quite active/aggressive. Calling 50 into a 150 pot requires ~25% equity, and 7-2s will be far behind vs a reasonable defending range — plus it’s a miserable hand to play OOP postflop. A min-raise to 200 as a steal is too risky against Flash’s 3-bet/pressure tendencies. Preserve the stack and fold.

I’m on the button with J♦8♦ — a playable suited one-gapper in position. With only the blinds in and a pot of 150 (to call 100), this is a good spot to open-raise and take the initiative: a standard 3x-ish open to 300 gives fold equity, builds a pot when we have position, and keeps the size manageable relative to my 9.2k stack. If I face a large 3-bet (e.g. ~900+ or a shove-sized reraise) I’ll fold; I’ll consider calling smaller 3-bets depending on odds and implied playability. If called, I’ll c-bet on favorable, dry flops and use position to control pot size and exploit weaker ranges.

I am in the Small Blind holding 8h 3d, which is a very weak starting hand. Minni has raised to 300 from the Button. Defending this hand out of position is not profitable, and there is no reason to attempt a bluff raise here. I will fold and wait for a better opportunity.

Preflop in the big blind facing a button raise to 300 after SB fold. My hand is Kc4d, offsuit king-high with a terrible kicker, no straight or flush potential. Button ranges are wide but still crush this hand on average. Poor playability OOP, bad implied odds if I hit king. Pot odds are 200 to call into 450 (~2.25:1), but equity is too low (~30-35% vs loose button open). Standard fold to preserve stack for better spots with 76BB effective.

I am on the button with Jack-Six offsuit. While a three-handed game allows for a wide opening range from the button, Jack-Six is at the very bottom of that range. My opponents, particularly Minni, have been active and willing to defend their blinds or three-bet. With a significant stack lead and only 16 hands remaining, I see little value in playing a marginal hand that often gets dominated or forced to fold post-flop. I will preserve my stack and wait for a better spot.

Flash folded from the button, leaving me heads-up against Minni in the big blind. KJ offsuit is a strong hand in this spot, roughly top 15-20% range, with good playability, straight potential, and domination potential vs calling ranges. From small blind vs big blind, standard strategy is to raise frequently to steal the dead money (150 in pot already). Raising to 3x the big blind (300 total) is a good size: builds the pot when ahead, applies pressure, and denies equity to weaker hands. Folding is too tight; calling/limping invites a raise and plays out of position post-flop awkwardly. With deep stacks (7600 effective vs Minni's 9260), value and initiative matter.

Elon opened to 300 from the SB and the pot is 400; I need to add 200 to call. I have A♦5♠ — an Ace with a weak kicker. Heads-up in the BB I'm out of position postflop, so I want to keep the pot manageable and realize equity rather than 3‑bet light OOP. Calling 200 to win ~600 gives ~3:1 pot odds (need ~25% equity), and against a typical SB opening range A5o has reasonable equity and the Ace blocker is useful. I’ll call to see a flop and play cautiously postflop (look to continue when I pair the Ace or pick up equity; fold to heavy aggression/4‑bets preflop).

Preflop as SB raiser to 300 with KJo, which is a strong hand vs BB calling range. Flop 2c2s7d is a dry, paired board that heavily favors my range: I have most Ax, Kx, Qx, overpairs, and sets with 22/77. Villain's BB calling range includes more speculative hands like 65s, 87s, Ax suited low, small pairs, suited connectors, which have little showdown value and draws are capped. No flush draw possible yet. KJ blocks some Kx value and has king overcard for SDV if called. OOP c-bet standard to deny equity to villain's float/calling hands, protect vs turns bringing 3-6-8-9-T etc. Size 350 (~60% pot) balances value and bluffs, keeps SPR manageable (~15:1 after), induces folds from marginal hands. Check risks giving free cards to draws/OESD potential, and villain can rep our range IP. Deep stacks allow profitable bluff.

Elon opened from the SB to 300 and I called preflop with A♦5♠ to keep the pot controllable. Flop 2♣–2♠–7♦ and Elon leads for 350; the pot is 950 so calling 350 gives me ~27% pot odds (I’d need roughly that equity to continue). A5o has reasonable equity vs a standard SB c‑betting range (overcards, pairs, some missed broadways), and my Ace blocks a number of strong Ax combos. I have no strong draws and I’m out of position, so a raise here would be a thin, high‑variance bluff. Folding is too weak given the price and my ability to improve. I’ll call 350 to realize equity, keep pot control, and plan to fold to large turn/river aggression unless I pair the Ace or otherwise improve.

Turn 9d completes potential diamond flush draws (7d9d on board) and some unlikely straight draws (e.g., T8 now 789T but no 8/T), while pairing possible 9x in villain's BB calling range (A9s, K9s, suited connectors). My KcJh remains king-high air: no pair, no strong draw, minimal equity (~20-30% vs her range). Preflop raise + flop c-bet represents strong OOP range (overpairs QQ+, AK/AQ, sets 22/77, KQ/KT), but she called flop IP, capping her range somewhat to pairs, 2x/Ax suited low, speculative hands. Betting OOP now has low fold equity vs stationy Minni (history shows frequent calls, esp. multi-street), risks calls/raises by 99/97/T9/77/22/draws. Size would need to be large (~800-1300) for fold equity, but stacks deep (~7k eff), bad risk/reward. Checking best: induces bluffs/value bets from her floaters/missed draws (she reps weak), pot control OOP, king-high showdown value vs bluffs/A-high, keeps SPR low for river jam if needed. History shows Minni passive IP often (checks back), so free showdown likely. Preserve stack late in 150-hand game.

Elon c‑bet the flop and I called there to keep the pot controllable with A♦5♠. On the turn (2♣–2♠–7♦–9♦) he checks after his flop aggression. I have only Ace‑high and no real draw — the Ad is a blocker to a lot of Ax combos but doesn’t give me showdown strength. Betting out here would be a thin, high‑variance bluff out of position that can easily get raised or called by better pairs (7x, 9x, pockets) and I don’t want to bloat the pot with a hand that needs to improve. Checking controls the pot size, lets me realize my equity cheaply, and keeps the option to fold to significant river aggression if I miss. Plan for river: fold to large value bets unless I pair the Ace or get a small, non‑committal bet that I can call for pot odds. Therefore I check back the turn to see a cheap river.

River 5c on a 2c2s7d9d board completes some unlikely club flushes (needing AcXc specifically) and gutshots, but my KcJh is still king-high no pair no draw. Preflop raise + flop c-bet + turn check represents strong hands like overpairs QQ+, sets 22/77, two-pair combos, but her BB call-flop call-check suggests medium strength (pairs like 22/77/T9/95/Ax suited diamonds/clubs) or floats. Betting river as bluff OOP vs stationy Minni (frequent multi-street calls per history) has poor fold equity; she'd call 1300+ pot-sized bet with any pair/draw/missed but decent SDV hands, and stacks deep (7k eff). Risk/reward bad late in game (134/150 hands). Checking optimal: pot control, king-high has showdown value vs her bluffs/floats/A-high, induces value bets from 77/22/95/T9/9x/87 she might bet thin, easy fold if bet big. Free showdown likely given her passive IP history. Preserves stack for better spots.

I improved to a one-pair (5s) on the river but it’s still a marginal, exploitable hand. Elon opened preflop and c‑bet the flop, then checked turn and checked the river — a line consistent with a wide, mostly missed c‑bet range (overcards, floats, some medium pairs) rather than a strong locked value hand. The hands that beat me (any 2 in hand, 7x/9x two‑pair, or higher pocket pairs) exist but are a smaller slice of his checking range. A small value bet (~1/3 pot) should get called by worse hands (A‑x, KQ, some small pairs/floaters) and fold out pure air, while not bloating the pot against the few combos that crush me. I will size down to extract thin value and keep my options: if Elon check‑raises I can credibly fold — this hand is not strong enough to call big aggression. Bet ~400 into 1300 to maximize thin value and fold to significant resistance.

River board 2c2s7d9d5c pairs deuces, possible diamond flush missed, minor club straight draws. KcJh king-high no pair/draw. Preflop raise + flop c-bet reps strong (overpairs QQ+, sets 22/77, Kx/Ax), turn/river check softens but she called flop IP (pairs 77/99/27/Ax/2x or draws), then small 400 bet (~23% pot) after my check-river OOP. Small size suggests thin value (77/99/T9/95/A-high) or bluff (missed AdXd, 87 etc.). History: Minni stationy IP, calls multi-street but bluffs occasionally (e.g., hand 68, 94). 4:1 odds (400 to call into 2100), king-high ~25-35% equity vs polarized range (beats bluffs/A-high, loses pairs+). Deep stacks (7k eff), low risk, +EV call for showdown value/pot control late (134/150). Raising poor (low FE vs sticky Minni, commits more). Fold too tight given odds/history.

9c2c is a speculative suited hand but very weak overall, with poor high-card strength and limited straight potential. Facing a limp from SB (Minni) and standard BB (Flash), calling 100 into 250 would require hitting big, which is unlikely. Position on button is nice for multiway pots, but this hand plays poorly postflop and doesn't warrant entering the pot. Opponents are aggressive from history, so better to fold and wait for premium hands to maximize stack long-term in this deep-stack cash game.

Heads-up in the SB I should be opening a very wide range to seize initiative. Tc2h is marginal but fine for a standard heads-up open — I want to apply pressure and pick up the pot preflop or get to a favorable flop. I’ll raise to the min (to 200 total) to keep the sizing small but give myself fold equity. Plan: fold to a sizable 3-bet with this hand, but if called I’ll continuation-bet dry boards and proceed cautiously out of position on wet cards. (Total committed = 200, which includes my 50 SB.)

I am in the Big Blind holding 3h 7d, which is an extremely weak starting hand. Minni has min-raised from the Small Blind. Although I am getting 3-to-1 pot odds and would have position post-flop, 37o is far too weak to defend profitably. It is best to fold and wait for a better spot.

I’m on the button with K♦4♠ — a weak K but in position against only the blinds. With the pot 150 and only 100 to call, the best long‑term play is to attempt a standard steal rather than limp or fold. I’ll open to 3x the big blind (300 total) to get decent fold equity against Flash (SB) and Elon (BB) while still being able to fold to a sizable 3‑bet. Plan: fold to a large 3‑bet, and if called play post‑flop in position — C‑bet dry, proceed cautiously on coordinated/ace‑high boards where I’m likely dominated. This sizing balances aggression and risk and maximizes expected value from late‑position steals.

I am in the Small Blind with 9-8 offsuit. Minni has raised to 300 from the Button. While 9-8 offsuit has some connectivity, playing it out of position against an active Button is generally a losing strategy. Elon, in the Big Blind, is also short-stacked enough to potentially jam or 3-bet, further complicating the decision. Folding here is the most prudent play to maintain my stack and wait for a higher-equity situation in the final hands of the game.

Q4o is a marginal offsuit hand with poor playability post-flop. Facing a button raise from Minni, who has been aggressive in late position throughout the history, my equity is insufficient even with pot odds of ~2.25:1 (pot 450, call 200). Folding preserves my 6500 stack for stronger hands, as calling commits chips to a tough spot out of position.

I am on the button with 2d 4c, which is one of the weakest starting hands in Texas Hold'em. Given that I have a substantial chip lead and we are nearing the end of the 150-hand session (Hand 137), there is no strategic reason to enter the pot with such a poor hand. Conserving my stack and waiting for better opportunities is the optimal play here.

Preflop, folded to SB vs BB. Hand is J8o, offsuit and marginal playability. Calling 50 into 150 pot gives 3:1 odds, but out of position postflop with a weak hand that struggles against BB's wide defending range. Minni defends frequently based on history. Raising as a steal is possible but risky OOP with poor hand. Folding maximizes EV long-term by avoiding tough spots.

3-handed on the button with Jd2d suited. Excellent position. J2s is a highly playable hand from the button in short-handed play due to its suited nature, potential for flush and wheel straight draws, and live cards. Standard opening range from BTN 3-handed includes over 60% of hands, and J2s fits easily. History shows aggressive opponents who often fold to steals. Unraised pot (assuming SB folded or limped minimally), pot 150, raise to 3x BB (300) to steal blinds and build pot in position if called. Deep effective stacks support speculative hands with position. Folding would be too tight late game; limping invites multiway pots OOP for blinds. Raising maximizes EV by denying equity and taking down dead money frequently.

Elon opened to 300 from the button. I’m in the small blind with Jc5s — offsuit, weak and often dominated. Calling 250 to play out of position (and likely multiway if the big blind defends) gives me poor post‑flop prospects and a negative‑EV spot. 3‑betting is possible only with strong blockers or a clear read that the button folds a lot; I don’t have that and Flash (BB) is capable of aggressive 3‑bets. With deep stacks this hand isn’t worth the risk. Fold preflop and wait for a better spot.

K7s is a strong starting hand to defend from the Big Blind against a Button open. I am getting excellent pot odds (3.25 to 1) to see a flop. Elon has been opening frequently from late positions, and my hand has good equity and post-flop playability. Calling is the most standard and effective play here to realize my equity without over-inflating the pot unnecessarily.

I am in the Big Blind and the board comes Queen of Spades, Eight of Diamonds, and Jack of Hearts. This board hits the Button's opening range quite well, as it contains many high cards and connectors. I have King high with a backdoor heart flush draw. Standard play here is to check to the pre-flop aggressor to see how they react. I do not have enough of a hand to lead into the raiser.

Flop Qs8dJh gives me top pair with Jd2d, a solid but kicker-weak holding from my button raising range. Flash's BB call pre-flop is wide, and his check suggests weakness or trapping, but checks from BB OOP often indicate marginal hands or draws. Board has straight draw potential (T9, 97) and backdoor flush draws, so betting protects my hand and charges them. Range advantage as pre-flop raiser; I can value bet vs worse pairs (Qx, worse Jx), floats, and draws while denying equity. Pot 650, standard c-bet ~50-60% (325-400) builds pot OOP and keeps pressure. Deep stacks allow for post-flop flexibility in position. Checking back risks giving free cards to overcards/draws and misses value. Betting maximizes EV long-term.

I have King-high with a backdoor heart flush draw on a very coordinated board [Qs 8d Jh] that hits the pre-flop raiser's button range significantly. Elon has bet 400 into 650. Given the texture, it is highly likely Elon has at least a piece of this board or a strong draw. Calling out of position with only backdoor equity and one overcard is not profitable in the long run. I will fold and wait for a better spot.

Holding K♥2♥ on the button is too weak to invest here. With blinds posted the pot is 150 and a call costs 100, so I’d need ~40% equity (100/(150+100)) to justify a call vs the blinds — K2s doesn’t have that vs two defending ranges and is easily dominated when I pair my king. Opening to try and steal risks a 3-bet from an aggressive SB (Flash) or a competent BB (Elon) and I don’t want to play a bloated pot postflop with this hand. Preserve stack and wait for a better spot — fold.

I am in the Small Blind with Queen-Trey offsuit. While it is a blind versus blind situation, this hand is extremely weak and has very poor playability post-flop, especially since I will be out of position for the remainder of the hand. Given that I have a significant chip lead and we are nearing the end of the session, there is no reason to take unnecessary risks or enter a pot with such a marginal hand. Folding is the most disciplined choice.

On the button in a three-handed game, I have a wide opening range. 53s is a speculative suited connector that is playable as a steal attempt. Given my chip lead and the proximity to the end of the session (hand 140 of 150), applying pressure on the blinds is a standard strategy. I will raise to 300 to try and take down the blinds or play a pot in position.

K5o is a marginal offsuit hand with poor playability, especially out of position in the small blind facing a button raise. Flash raises frequently from the button, indicating a wide range, but K5o lacks strong equity, blockers, or suited/connectivity to defend profitably. Folding preserves stack for better spots, as post-flop OOP decisions would be difficult with this hand against likely aggression from Flash and potential squeeze from Minni in BB.

Facing a standard button open to 300 while I’m in the big blind with 5♣8♥ (offsuit). This hand is low, disconnected and has very poor playability out of position. I’d need ~31% equity to break even calling 200 into the 450 pot, and versus a button’s opening range my equity and post‑flop prospects are both weak. 3‑betting here would be reckless with junk and I’d be out of position in a bloated pot. Preserve stack and wait for a better spot — fold.

84o is a speculative trash hand with no high card value, poor connectivity, and offsuit. Facing a 100 to call into a 150 pot (pot odds ~2.5:1 pre-call, ~1.5:1 effective), the hand has minimal equity multiway or heads-up against typical calling ranges from blinds. On button, even stealing is marginal with this holding; folding preserves stack for premium spots in late tournament phase (hand 141/150). Opponent stacks deep, no need to gamble.

7♥2♦ is the worst possible starting hand — garbage offsuit with virtually no playability. I’m in the small blind and would be out of position post‑flop against the big blind, and calling 50 to see a flop is a negative‑EV proposition vs a competent opponent with deep stacks. No reason to invest more chips (raising is clearly wrong here). Preserve the stack for real spots and fold.

2♥9♥ is a very weak hand despite being suited. Pot is 150 and it costs 100 to play (you'd need ~40% equity to justify a call into 250), but vs two opponents my equity is far below that and will be even worse multiway or if facing a 3‑bet. Both opponents have shown willingness to open and 3‑bet and the big blind (Elon) has ~67BB — he can apply pressure. Raising to try to steal invites 3‑bets and flipping dominated postflop spots; limping multiway is low EV. With a healthy 10510 stack I should conserve chips for better spots on the button. Fold.

I am in the Small Blind with T3o after the Button folded. This is a very weak hand to play out of position against the Big Blind. Given my significant chip lead and the fact that there are only a few hands left in the game, there is no reason to risk chips with a bottom-tier hand. Folding is the most prudent play to preserve my stack.

I am on the button with Jc 3h, which is a very weak hand. I am currently the chip leader in this three-way game. Folding is the standard play here as this hand has poor playability and I want to preserve my stack as we approach the end of the session.

Preflop in SB vs BB only after Button folds. Hand Tc6s is offsuit, poor connectivity, low playability OOP postflop. Calling 50 into 200 pot (pot odds ~4:1) but likely dominated by BB's wide defending range. Raising OOP with weak hand lacks fold equity and strong blockers. BB (Minni) is aggressive but folding trash preserves stack for premium spots late in 150-hand game with 68BB effective stack. Optimal: fold.

3-handed on the button with KTs suited, a strong premium hand with great playability, position, and suited broadway potential. Blinds total 150 in pot, no action yet, so standard open-raise to 3x the big blind (300 total) to take initiative, build the pot with a strong range, and apply pressure on the blinds. My stack of 6800 allows for flexible post-flop play, and opponents have shown aggression but KTs performs well against their calling ranges.

Elon opened to 300 from the button. In the small blind with K♦J♥ I have a hand worth defending but I'm out of position and there is a big blind behind who can come along. Folding is too tight and 3‑betting to isolate is too risky OOP — it bloats the pot and I can be dominated by stronger hands. Calling keeps the pot controlled, gets correct pot odds (250 into 450 → ~35.7% immediate pot odds) and lets me see a flop where KJ has reasonable equity against a wide button open. Plan: call, evaluate flop — continue with top pair / strong draws, fold to heavy pressure when missing.

I am in the Big Blind with Ten-Deuce offsuit. Elon raised from the Button, and Minni called from the Small Blind. Although I am getting 3.5:1 pot odds to call, Ten-Deuce is an extremely weak hand that performs poorly post-flop, especially out of position against two opponents. Given that we are near the end of the session (hand 144 of 150) and I have a significant stack lead, there is no need to defend with such a marginal hand and risk losing more chips on later streets.

Flop: A♠ 7♣ A♥ — I hold K♦ J♥ with no pair. I called preflop to keep the pot manageable, but on this paired-A board I’m out of position and behind virtually any Ace and many of the hands the button opened (A‑x, pocket pairs, even slow‑played strong hands). Pot is 700 and there’s no bet yet. Best line is pot control: check and let the preflop raiser act. Betting here as a bluff OOP is expensive and has poor fold equity versus an opener’s range; check also keeps bluffs/turn cards available and preserves my stack. If Elon bets, plan is to fold to a standard c‑bet (I have very little showdown equity vs his continuing range) and only consider calling very tiny probes (~≤1/4 pot) if I have a specific read that he c‑bets extremely wide. If checked to on the turn I’ll re-evaluate and consider a delayed bluff only on clean, favorable turn cards. Action: check.

Flop of As 7c Ah is extremely dry and ace-heavy, hitting my pre-flop raising range hard (AA, AK, AQ, AJ, A10, pocket pairs). Minni's SB call range is wide but includes many Ax hands, pairs, suited connectors, and broadways. She checks, showing potential weakness or a trap with a strong ace. My KTs suited gives me king-high (live king blocker to AK), backdoor straight draw, and a strong 10-heart flush draw (10 outs to the nut flush, ~20% equity). Position is excellent. C-bet half-pot as a semi-bluff for thin value against worse aces/draws and fold equity against bluffs/misses; protects against her improving; builds pot for fold equity later streets. Pot 700 allows flexible sizing; 400 is balanced, represents strong ace, and my stack (6500) supports it. Opponents aggressive historically, but this spot exploits her check.

I checked the flop as planned — A♠ 7♣ A♥ is a horrible board for K♦J♥. I have no pair and essentially no realistic draws (no backdoor flush/straight possibilities), so I’m behind virtually every Ace and most of Elon's value/continuation range. Pot is 1100 and he bet 400; calling 400 to win 1500 gives ~26.7% pot odds, but my true equity vs his continuing range here is well below that. Raising as a bluff OOP is too risky (no fold equity and poor blockers). Fold and preserve my stack for a better spot.

I’m on the button with A♦3♥ in a 3-handed spot — only the SB and BB left. This is a clear steal/pressure spot: A3o is marginal multiway but perfectly reasonable to open from the button.
